items.descriptionHtml:
We’re looking to hire a Manager, Product Management for AI/ML Model Governance to join the Machine Learning Experience (MLX) team!


At Capital One India, we work in a fast paced and intellectually rigorous environment to solve fundamental business problems at scale. Using advanced analytics, data science and machine learning, we derive valuable insights about product and process design, consumer behavior, regulatory and credit risk, and more from large volumes of data, and use it to build cutting edge patentable products that drive the business forward. Product Management at Capital One is a critical, vibrant craft that requires a blend of entrepreneurial drive, keen business acumen, customer experience obsession, technical depth and strong people leadership.


The MLX team is at the forefront of how Capital One builds and deploys responsible GenAI and ML models; we onboard and educate associates on the GenAI and ML platforms and products that the whole company uses; we drive new innovation and research; and we’re working to seamlessly infuse Generative AI and ML into the fabric of the company. The AI experience we're creating is the foundation upon which our lines of business will continue to deliver next-generation machine learning-driven products and services for our customers.


Specific responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

  • Develop and communicate the product vision and strategy for your area of responsibility
  • Deeply understand the needs of data scientists, machine learning engineers and model risk officers across Capital One
  • Partner with business and technology leadership to align on prioritization of key problems to be solved to maximize business and customer outcomes
  • Collaborate with teams in India and USA to drive alignment with different teams working on the platform
  • Incorporate design thinking and analytics to inform product design
  • Maintain a healthy backlog of work and play a critical role in many agile ceremonies.
  • Support the team with escalation and resolution of impediments
  • Serve as the connection between Customers, Capital One’s mission, and Tech. Partners


We want you if you are:

  • Interested in building lasting relationships with peers and having fun while building products that delight customers and drive value to the business.
  • You enjoy putting yourself in the shoes of customers to identify their pain points and then collaborating with cross-functional teams to solve these customer challenges. Even better if you have experience doing this for data scientists and machine learning engineers.
  • You are a learner who is able to quickly get up to speed in a highly technical environment.
  • Passionate about building a well-rounded product management skill set that is human centered, business focused, and technology driven with an emphasis on integrated problem solving and people leadership.
  • You are experienced working in an Agile environment.
  • You are a do-er who can also zoom out and think strategically.
  • You have excellent oral and written communication skills.
  • You are highly collaborative and have the ability to influence and lead.


Basic Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s in Engineering or Technology.
  • At least 8 years of work experience in tech industry
  • At least 5 years of experience in product management.
  • At least 3 years of experience in building Enterprise AI/ML platform products or AI/ML powered products for enterprises.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Expertise in building enterprise platform products with focus on AI/ML Model Governance, AI/ML Model Risk Management and Automation OR should have worked on LLM-powered co-pilot, agent assist capabilities.
  • Experience with Generative AI and Machine Learning technologies.
  • Experience in Data Science or Business Analytics.


At this time, Capital One will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

items.descriptionHtml:
Our mission at Duolingo is to develop the best education in the world and make it universally available. It’s a big mission, and that’s where you come in!

At Duolingo, you’ll join a team that cares about educating our users, experimenting with big ideas, making fact-based decisions, and finding innovative solutions to complex problems. You’ll have limitless learning opportunities and daily collaborations with world-class minds — while doing work that’s both meaningful and fun.

Join our life-changing mission to develop education for our half a billion (and growing!) learners around the world.

Read our blog to learn more.

About The Role...

As a Group Product Manager, you’ll be a key product leader in the Growth Area. The Growth Area is responsible for growing Duolingo’s user base by making our app so fun, delightful, and effective that our learners stick around, make real progress, and tell their friends all about Duolingo!

🧠 You will...

  • Define the product strategy to make Duolingo a daily habit for millions of learners in India and the Asian Pacific region
  • Identify and execute on product opportunities in partnership with engineering, design, marketing, strategy, localization, and other functions
  • Drive product features through their entire creative cycle: ideation, specification, experimentation, analysis, launch, and iteration
  • Surface insights from quantitative and qualitative data to inform product decisions and champion Indian and APAC learners
  • Communicate with global teams across time zones
  • Lead and grow a product team

✅ You have...

  • A track record of driving impact as an individual contributor on a consumer product team
  • Experience leading and growing small teams of product managers
  • High autonomy, resourcefulness, and creativity
  • Excellent spoken and written communication skills
  • Strong data, design, and product sense
  • A desire to make the world a better place through technology and education

Take a peek at how we care for our employees' holistic well-being with our benefits here.

We will do everything we can within reason to make sure that your interview takes place in an environment that fairly and accurately assesses your skills. If you need assistance or accommodation, please contact accommodations@duolingo.com.

Duolingo is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

By applying for this position your data will be processed as per the Duolingo Applicant Privacy Notice.

Unfortunately, there is a rise in scammers pretending to be real Duolingo employees. Duolingo and our employees will never ask for your Social Security number, bank details, or passport info, and we’ll never ask you to deposit a check, purchase equipment, or exchange money during the interview process. Real Duolingo employees always use an email that ends in @duolingo.com or @recruiting.duolingo.com. Stay alert and double-check these details before sharing any information.

Sign up for job alerts here.

items.descriptionHtml:
About Albertsons Companies Inc. (ACI):

As a leading food and drug retailer in the United States, Albertsons Companies, Inc. operates over 2,200 stores across 35 states and the District of Columbia. Our well-known banners across the United States, including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Jewel-Osco and others, serve more than 36 million U.S customers each week.

We build and shape technology solutions that solve customers’ problems every day, making things easier for them when they shop with us online or in a store. We have made bold, strategic moves to migrate and modernize our core foundational capabilities, positioning ourselves as the first fully cloud-based grocery tech company in the industry.

Our success is built on a one-team approach, driven by the desire to understand and enhance the customer experience. By constantly pushing the boundaries of retail, we are transforming shopping into an experience that is easy, efficient, fun and engaging.


About Albertsons India Capability Center:

At Albertsons India Capability Center, we're not just pushing the boundaries of technology and retail innovation, we're cultivating a space where ideas flourish and careers thrive. Our workplace in India is a vital extension of the Albertsons Companies Inc. workforce and important to the next phase in the company’s technology journey to support millions of customers’ lives every day.

At the Albertsons India Capability Center, we are raising the bar to grow across Technology & Engineering, AI, Digital and other company functions, and transform a 165-year-old American retailer. At Albertsons India Capability Center, associates collaborate directly with international teams, enhancing decision-making processes and organizational agility through exciting and pivotal projects. Your work will make history and help millions of lives each day come together around the joys of food and inspire their well-being.


Group Product Manager

About the Role:

The Marketing Product Management team has broad responsibility to leverage digital marketing and advertising technologies to build best in class, omnichannel communication tools and customer experiences to maximize return on investment and grow customer lifetime value and retention.


Key responsibilities:

Required Skills:

  • The Group Product Manager for Marketing will play a strategic leadership role in key areas of the overall marketing product vision; providing tactical guidance for a team of product managers in organizing, defining, prioritizing, and delivering features for targeted and 1:1 personalized marketing campaign.
  • Their expertise in product management, combined with deep understanding of the retail and grocery industry, will be instrumental in enhancing our digital marketing campaigns, as we continue down our transformation towards 1:1 marketing.
  • She/he will define the product vision and roadmap for their product sub pillar to achieve the business KPIs. She/he will be responsible for collecting and interpreting user research and data, conceiving innovative products, creating a robust product roadmap, and managing the product lifecycle.
  • The right candidate will be a strategic and ambitious visionary with the proven ability to deliver products that create impact and deliver exceptional value to our associates, vendors partners who are deeply focused in serving our guest shopping omni channel.
  • This leader should be highly motivated to succeed and influence his/her team and stakeholders to do so, in an approachable manner, that engenders trust and confidence at all levels across the organization.
  • This role will also be looked upon to provide expertise on larger and more complex initiatives and projects that overlap between teams within the Marketing Product portfolio.
  • To be successful in this role, you must have a strong customer obsession, strategic mindset, critical thinking, bias for action, data savvy, visual storytelling, technical proficiency and comfort with modern cloud native marketing and advertising tech stacks
  • Must have extensive experience and knowledge in retail Marketing, eCommerce, Digital knowledge
  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, business, or related. Master’s degree or MBA preferred.
  • 8+ years of product management experience in digital marketing, eCommerce, and/or retail-facing experience
  • Passionate about building great products that scale, and solving customer and business problems
  • Data-driven/analytical mindset, strong prioritization and negotiating skills.
  • Excellent stakeholder management skills and ability to align cross-functional teams around a cohesive product vision.
  • Strong technical ability to translate user needs into requirements, user stories and development of acceptance criteria in an Agile environment.
  • Proven track record of designing and delivering delightful customer experiences that create customer and business value.
  • Demonstrated ability to create an overarching product vision, using data and analysis to support strategy and prioritization, and day to day product execution.
  • Proven ability to develop product strategies and effectively communicate recommendations to executive management.
  • Successful track record leading the full product lifecycle from strategy to execution for customer-facing digital capabilities.
  • Experience working with both onshore and offshore engineering teams to build features and ensure quality of delivery, including sprint planning, product backlog management, technical sizing of tasks, and feature prioritization.
  • Skilled at working effectively with cross functional teams, and influencing conversations to drive positive outcomes for the product and the business.
  • Understanding market trends, customer needs, and competitive landscape, shaping the product roadmap and strategy accordingly.
  • Improving the team in some subset of product management practices, vision, creativity, ownership, hiring, mentoring, and team culture.
  • You should have demonstrable track record for innovation and produce value at cross sections of people, process and technology, with empathy for end users.

items.descriptionHtml:
Ralph Lauren Corporation (NYSE:RL) is a global leader in the design, marketing and distribution of premium lifestyle products in five categories: apparel, accessories, home, fragrances, and hospitality. For more than 50 years, Ralph Lauren's reputation and distinctive image have been consistently developed across an expanding number of products, brands and international markets. The Company's brand names, which include Ralph Lauren, Ralph Lauren Collection, Ralph Lauren Purple Label, Polo Ralph Lauren, Double RL, Lauren Ralph Lauren, Polo Ralph Lauren Children, Chaps, among others, constitute one of the world's most widely recognized families of consumer brands.

At Ralph Lauren, we unite and inspire the communities within our company as well as those in which we serve by amplifying voices and perspectives to create a culture of belonging, ensuring inclusion, and fairness for all. We foster a culture of inclusion through: Talent, Education & Communication, Employee Groups and Celebration.

,

Ralph Lauren is seeking a passionate Product Manager to drive the discovery, documentation, and execution of the functional and system requirements. This role will be responsible for understanding business needs and challenges and working with key stakeholders. This role will be an integral part of the digital team to communicate and refine requirements towards the goal of designing and delivering both technical as well as consumer-centric solutions. Experience with e-commerce, and in-store consumer experience is desired as this would benefit in delivering solutions that contribute to the success of the business.

,

  • Gather and document business and functional requirements by leveraging discovery sessions, requirement workshops, document analysis, use cases, user stories, and workflow analysis.
  • Translate high-level information into digestible details and specifications, as well as abstract up from low-level information to a general understanding.
  • Critically evaluate information from multiple sources in order to reconcile conflicts, align to clear goals, influence product direction, and drive solutions.
  • Proactively communicate with external and internal customers to analyze the feasibility of new features and enhancement, understand information needs, as well as capture requirements.
  • Work closely with a cross-functional, dynamic, and multi-disciplinary team of business, store associates, architects, developers, and subject matter experts to establish the technical vision, analyze tradeoffs, and actively mitigate potential risks.
  • Determine operational performance via data analysis in order to measure success against goals and identify root causes of complex problems.
  • Assist with the rollout of new functionality and user acceptance testing, ensuring that product stipulations are satisfied, issues are resolved, and exceptional user experiences are delivered.
  • Ensure end users have the training and documentation required to efficiently manage new features.
  • Partner with business teams in documentation of user manual and training document for users of the application
  • Provide support and triage complex issues, outlining the impact and potential resolutions to stakeholders while working with the development team to implement the best solution.
  • Assist with the rollout of new functionality and the execution of test plans to ensure that product stipulations are satisfied, issues are resolved, and exceptional user experiences are delivered

,

  • Bachelor’s degree, computer science or similar technology degree.
  • Product management experience with a track record of delivering high quality results.
  • Experience writing and vetting business requirement documentation, functional and system requirements, user stories, process workflows, and test cases is desired.
  • Analytical and product development skills with a thorough understanding of how to interpret customer and business needs, translating them to application and operational requirements.
  • Excellent communication/writing skills as well as the ability to work effectively on multiple projects simultaneously and across internal and external organizations.
  • Comfortable in a highly-matrixed, iterative development environment and good at balancing collaboration and advocacy. Able to work independently as well as under direction of product and project leaders.

The power we create together – when we combine your strengths with ours – is unstoppable. The center leverages the power of data and analytics to drive growth for critical business functions such as operations, finance, people, and technology. The teams are transforming Operations through Tech and Analytics.Do You Dream Big?We Need You.Job DescriptionJob Title: Senior Manager – Finance Transformation (Global Product Owner – ATR & OTC)Location: Bangalore (Onsite)Reporting to: Senior Director – Finance Operations & Internal ControlsPURPOSE OF ROLE This role drives global finance transformation across Record‑to‑Report (ATR) and Order‑to‑Cash (OTC) processes by defining product vision, standardizing processes, and enabling touchless, compliant, and scalable finance operations. The role ensures that finance transformation initiatives deliver measurable business value through process simplification, automation, strong controls, and alignment with global finance strategy and items.descriptionHtml:
Dreaming big is in our DNA. It’s who we are as a company. It’s our culture. It’s our heritage. And more than ever, it’s our future. A future where we’re always looking forward. Always serving up new ways to meet life’s moments. A future where we keep dreaming bigger. We look for people with passion, talent, and curiosity, and provide them with the teammates, resources and opportunities to unleash their full potential. The power we create together – when we combine your strengths with ours – is unstoppable. Are you ready to join a team that dreams as big as you do?


AB InBev GCC was incorporated in 2014 as a strategic partner for Anheuser-Busch InBev. The center leverages the power of data and analytics to drive growth for critical business functions such as operations, finance, people, and technology. The teams are transforming Operations through Tech and Analytics.


Do You Dream Big?


We Need You.


Job Description

Job Title: Senior Manager – Finance Transformation (Global Product Owner – ATR & OTC)

Location: Bangalore (Onsite)

Reporting to: Senior Director – Finance Operations & Internal Controls


PURPOSE OF ROLE

This role drives global finance transformation across Record‑to‑Report (ATR) and Order‑to‑Cash (OTC) processes by defining product vision, standardizing processes, and enabling touchless, compliant, and scalable finance operations. The role ensures that finance transformation initiatives deliver measurable business value through process simplification, automation, strong controls, and alignment with global finance strategy and policies.


KEY TASKS AND ACCOUNTABILITIES

  • Define and lead the global product vision and roadmap for ATR & OTC finance transformation initiatives aligned with the themes of Touchless, Standard, and Compliant.
  • Drive end‑to‑end ownership of finance transformation programs, from problem definition through solution design, delivery, and value realization.
  • Collaborate with Zone Finance, Shared Service Centers, Global Process Owners, and Technology teams to gather requirements while avoiding local deviations from global standards.
  • Prioritize and manage the product backlog to ensure delivery of the highest‑value features aligned with business priorities and global finance policies.
  • Translate business needs into executable solutions by defining user stories, feature requirements, and acceptance criteria in partnership with business analysts and SMEs.
  • Lead stakeholder engagement across country, zone, and global levels to ensure alignment, buy‑in, and successful adoption of transformation outcomes.
  • Plan and coordinate releases with Corporate Technology teams to ensure timely, high‑quality deployment of finance capabilities.
  • Own the financial governance of transformation initiatives, including business case development, annual planning submissions, and budget adherence.
  • Monitor transformation outcomes using defined KPIs and take corrective actions to ensure target performance is achieved.
  • Identify resistance to change and actively drive change management actions to embed standardized, best‑in‑class finance processes.
  • Provide regular executive updates to senior leadership (VPs, Sr. Directors) on progress, risks, outcomes, and value delivered.


QUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE, SKILLS

Education:

  • Master’s degree in finance, Accounting, Business, Technology, or a related field from a Premium Institute preferred


Experience:

  • 8–12+ years of experience in Finance Operations, Finance Transformation, Process Improvement, or Product Ownership.
  • Strong background in Accounting, Shared Service Center operations, and global finance process standardization.
  • Experience working in SOX‑controlled environments with exposure to internal audit and internal controls.


Technical/Functional Skills:

Mandatory Skills:

  • Finance Process Transformation (ATR / OTC) – Expert
  • Product Ownership & Backlog Management – Advanced
  • Global Stakeholder Management & Influence – Expert
  • Process Standardization & Controls Design – Advanced
  • Business Case & Financial Planning – Advanced
  • SAP Finance (ECC / S4 data model & processes) – Advanced
  • Agile / Scrum Delivery Models – Intermediate


Preferred (Good to have) Skills:

  • Automation & Digital Finance Solutions – Intermediate
  • Generative AI / Agentic AI exposure (opportunities & risks) – Beginner–Intermediate
  • Process Mining & Analytics Platforms – Intermediate
  • Data Analytics & Visualization (Power BI, SQL, Python) – Intermediate
  • Change Management & Adoption Enablement – Intermediate


And above all of this, an undying love for beer!

We dream big to create a future with more cheers.

items.descriptionHtml:
Overview

At Zebra, we are a community of innovators who come together to create new ways of working. United by curiosity and a culture of caring, we develop smart solutions that anticipate our customer’s and partner’s needs and solve their challenges.

Being part of Zebra Nation means you are seen, heard, valued, and respected. Drawing from our unique perspectives, we collaborate to deliver on our purpose. Here you are part of a team pushing boundaries today to redefine the work of tomorrow for organizations, their employees, and those they serve.

You’ll have opportunities to learn and lead in a forward-thinking environment, defining your path to a fulfilling career while channeling your skills toward causes you care about—locally and globally.

Come make an impact every day at Zebra.

What We're Looking For

The Product Management team drives the development of product definition and planning to optimize the company's products and solutions. The team applies broad knowledge of company's product and solutions capabilities as well as the competitive environment to identify new growth areas, new technology and emerging product opportunities. Oversees the analysis and evaluation of key market statistics and customer requirements to define product strategies and identify product opportunities to expand market share.The Product Management team also evaluates in-depth market analysis and leads business case development for optimization of existing products and solutions alternatives and/or to create the next generation of new products. Collaborates with research and development to create products and validate early product definitions. This role is both strategic and technical in nature and requires meaningful industry experience.As a Senior Manager, Product Management, you will be responsible for a specific product line contribution to the overall business unit. This includes increasing the profitability of existing products to developing new products for the company. You will build products from existing ideas, and help to develop new ideas based on your industry experience and your contact with customers and prospects.

  • The position has responsibility for the growth and business success of an assigned product line life cycle and/or product category. The role is responsible for managing other product managers in both developing strategies and managing tactical execution of product growth and development plans as well as delivering management objectives, coaching, and mentoring
  • This position is responsible for ensuring the ongoing review of the competitive environment for his/her product category. This is accomplished through analysis of competitive equipment and compilation of other pertinent findings, and the information thus gained is published and/or presented on a regular basis
  • The Senior Manager, Product Management ensures that his/her organization works with the Product Marketing and Marketing Communications in developing product promotion programs. Product Management is responsible for insuring that positioning and factual content are correct, and that timing for release of promotional materials fits with the product launch schedule. In addition, product positioning and pricing strategies are owned and managed by the Senior Product Manager and team who coordinate the communication of this information within product marketing
  • Product Life Cycle Management
  • Work closely with operations and supply chain to properly manage: forecast, ramp plans, component obsolescence, misaligned sales orders, yield issues, and product hold issues.
  • Manage transitions and discontinuations of product line
  • Manage product related communications to both internal and external associates
  • The Senior Manager, Product Management actively supports the Sales organization as required in select sales presentations, proposals and opportunities. Responsible for competitive analysis, as represented in developed presentation materials including detailed metrics and analysis to support business case and product development process
  • Senior Manager, Product Management and his/her team work closely with internal stakeholders throughout the development process in negotiating design trade-offs that may be needed, while preserving the requirements stated in the market requirements document (MRD). Internal stakeholders include but not limited to product marketing, end users, channel partners, sales, engineering, support organizations, and manufacturing
  • Travel as needed to visit customers, partners, attend seminars, trade shows or meet area sales personnel

Preferred Education

  • Master's Degree

Preferred Work Experience (years)

  • 12+ years' experience, including at least 3 years managing multiple product managers in related discipline or technology industry

Key Skills And Competencies

  • Excellent interpersonal skills with strong ability to interact with customers
  • Strong communication skills including written, verbal and oral presentation skills as well as ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from senior management, managers, clients and customers
  • Ability to assess complex market landscape and create focused, integrated product plans
  • Attention to detail, and strong understanding of complex products and systems
  • Deep understanding of financial concepts including P&L, balances sheets, and ROI models with delivery of product through defined objectives
  • Multi-task and manage complex programs
  • Advanced market knowledge in assigned product vertical, category or technology or solutions
  • Highly creative with ability to think strategically and develop strategic/business plans
  • The successful candidate will be a self-starter; this individual must be adaptable, persuasive, tenacious and perceptive and be able to execute a well-developed plan
  • Strong planning and execution skills
  • Excellent skills in Microsoft business applications including Word, Excel and PowerPoint
  • Ability to travel
  • Candidates must have a proven track record managing products in the authentication/authorization space

Benefits

We understand the importance of work-life balance and wellbeing, which is why we offer flexibility for our teams including: hybrid work, adaptable hours, Summer Flex Fridays, Focus Fridays, and an annual companywide well-being day to promote revitalization and success.

Job Posting Statement

To protect candidates from falling victim to online fraudulent activity involving fake job postings and employment offers, please be aware our recruiters will always connect with you via @zebra.com email accounts. Applications are only accepted through our applicant tracking system and only accept personal identifying information through that system. Our Talent Acquisition team will not ask for you to provide personal identifying information via e-mail or outside of the system. If you are a victim of identity theft contact your local police department.

AI Technology Statement

Zebra Technologies leverages AI technology to evaluate job applications using objective, job-relevant criteria. This approach enhances efficiency and promotes fairness in the hiring process. However, every decision regarding interviews and hiring is made by our dedicated team, because we believe people make the best decisions about people. For more on how we use technology in hiring and how we process applicant data, see our Zebra Privacy Policy.

items.descriptionHtml:
🚀 Why Headout?

We're a rocketship: 9-figure revenue, record growth, and profitable

With $130M in revenue, guests in 100+ cities, and 18 months of profitability, Headout is the fastest-growing marketplace in the travel industry, and we're just getting started. We've raised $60M+ from top-tier investors and are building a durable company for the long term — because that's what our mission needs and deserves. We're growing, profitable and nowhere near done.

What we do is important

In an increasingly digital world, there is a desperate need to augment our human experience by getting us to interact with the real world around us and the people in it. At Headout, our mission is to be the easiest, fastest, and most delightful way to head out to a real-life experience — from immersive tours to museums to live events and everything in between.

Why now?

The foundation is strong. The opportunity ahead is even bigger. We've hit profitability, built momentum, and proven the model — but there's so much more to build. If you're looking to join a company where the trajectory is steep and your impact is real, this is the moment.

Our culture

Reinventing the travel industry isn't easy, but that's the fun part.

We care deeply about ownership, craft, and impact, and we're here to do the best work of our careers. We won't pretend like it's for everyone but if you're a builder who loves solving tough problems, you'll feel right at home.

👩‍💻 The Role

We're hiring a Group Product Manager (Growth) to join Headout's product leadership. You'll own the growth and customer experience charter, shaping how millions of travellers discover, fall in love with, and keep coming back to the world's most memorable real-world experiences.

You'll set the strategic direction for growth, translate it into a focused roadmap, and execute with speed and rigour. You'll sit at the crossroads of product strategy, experimentation culture, and cross-functional leadership, and you'll leave fingerprints on every meaningful metric across the funnel.

We're building toward 2x in two years and 5x in five. This role is how we get there.

🤩 What Makes This Role Special

  • Shape the Growth Strategy: Define the multi-horizon roadmap for growth - balancing near-term wins with foundational systems.
  • Drive Outsized Impact: Your decisions will influence millions of users and tens of millions in GMV.
  • Build a Culture of Experimentation: Institutionalise hypothesis-driven thinking across the team and the organisation.
  • Partner at the Leadership Table: Work directly with the Product, Design, Engineering leadership, and senior stakeholders to align priorities and resources.
  • Lead a Growth & Customer Experience Squad: Own and grow a team of PMs working across.

🌟 What You'll Bring

  • Deep understanding of your target user and the ability to translate insights into high-impact features.
  • Strong product sense—you know what great looks like, and how to get there.
  • Ability to break down complex problems, make trade-offs, and prioritize based on user and business impact.
  • Clear, structured communicator - whether it's writing PRDs or aligning stakeholders.
  • Comfortable coaching product managers and creating alignment across pods and functions.

🧠 What you will work on

  • Own the user conversion and post-booking experience charter end-to-end across web and app.
  • Define a coherent growth strategy that ladders up to company-level goals and communicates clearly to leadership.
  • Lead a small team of PMs - setting direction, coaching on craft, removing blockers, and creating conditions for high performance.
  • Build a rigorous experimentation programme: prioritisation frameworks, test velocity targets, and a culture of learning from failure.
  • Drive alignment across Product, Engineering, Design, Data, and Marketing on growth priorities and resource allocation.
  • Identify compounding growth levers - flywheel effects, platform bets, and structural improvements alongside tactical wins.
  • Define and hold the team accountable to OKRs, experiment velocity, and impact metrics across the funnel.
  • Represent the growth & customer experience charter in cross-functional planning cycles, including annual and quarterly roadmap reviews.

💝 What Skills & Experience You Need

  • Currently operating at Lead, Staff, Principal, or GPM level in consumer tech, marketplace, e-commerce, or travel.
  • Demonstrated track record of owning growth outcomes - discovery, conversion, retention. Strong strategic thinking: ability to zoom out to the 6 months to a 2 year horizon while maintaining operational clarity on the next 90 days.
  • Deep experimentation fluency - not just running A/B tests, but building the systems, cadence, and culture around them.
  • Excellent analytical skills: comfortable with funnel analysis, cohort analysis, and using data to both identify opportunities and measure impact.
  • Experience influencing without authority: aligning Engineering, Design, and Data teams on shared priorities in a fast-moving environment.
  • Strong written and verbal communication - able to craft clear strategy docs, PRDs, and leadership narratives.
  • Experience hiring, onboarding, and developing PM talent is a strong plus.

EEO statement

At Headout, we don't just accept differences — we celebrate it, we support it, and we thrive on it for the benefit of our employees, our partners, and the community at large. Headout provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age or disability. During the interview process, if you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, you may contact the recruiter assigned to your application or email us at life@headout.com.

Privacy policy

Please note that once you apply for this job profile your personal data will be retained for a period of one (1) year. Headout shall process this data for recruitment purposes only. Once the relevant job profile is filled or once the time period of one (1) year from the date of the job application has passed, whichever is later, Headout shall either delete your data or inform you that it shall keep it in its database for future roles. In compliance with the relevant privacy laws, you have the right to request access to your personal data, to request that your personal data be rectified or erased, and to request that the processing of your personal data be restricted. If you have any concerns or questions about the way Headout handle your data, you can contact our Data Protection Officer for more information.

items.descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Group Product Manager / Associate Director


About the Role:

We are looking for a Group Product Manager / Associate Director to lead business-critical product charters and drive durable outcomes at scale.


This role goes beyond individual features or isolated initiatives — you will shape product strategy, identify leverage points across the user journey, and ensure execution translates into measurable business impact.


You will work closely with senior business leaders while partnering deeply with Engineering, Analytics, Design, CX, Sales, and Marketing teams.


This role requires strong business judgment, systems thinking, user empathy, and the ability to operate confidently in ambiguity.


What You Will Be Doing:

Strategic Ownership :

● Define and evolve product strategy aligned with business goals

● Identify high-leverage opportunities across growth, retention, and monetisation

● Translate company-level goals into clear product direction and priorities

● Balance short-term execution with long-term platform thinking

User Thinking & Product Judgment :

● Develop a deep understanding of employer and job-seeker needs, behaviours, and pain points

● Ensure product decisions are grounded in real user outcomes, not just metrics

● Translate user insights into scalable product strategies and clear value propositions

● Advocate for user impact while balancing business constraints

Business & Product Leadership :

● Own outcomes across multiple initiatives rather than individual features

● Connect product decisions to measurable business impact

● Partner with business teams to ensure product strategy reflects market realities

● Influence cross-functional priorities and drive alignment at leadership level

Execution & Systems Thinking :

● Ensure high-quality execution through clarity of goals, scope, and success criteria

● Drive experimentation and validate major bets through data

● Convert repeated operational challenges into scalable product systems

● Anticipate risks and proactively adjust strategy

Data & Decision Making :

● Define analytical frameworks that guide decision-making

● Use data fluently for prioritisation, storytelling, and strategic choices

● Distinguish meaningful outcomes from vanity indicators

Cross-Functional Leadership :

● Drive alignment across Product, Engineering, Analytics, Design, CX, Sales, and Marketing

● Create clarity through ambiguity and resolve trade-offs constructively

● Lead through influence rather than authority


What You Will Need:

● 8–12+ years of Product Management experience

● Proven track record of driving meaningful business outcomes through product

● Experience owning large product areas or business-critical charters

● Strong product strategy and systems-thinking capability

● Demonstrated ability to combine user insight with business judgment

● Deep data fluency and experimentation mindset

● Strong business acumen across growth, retention, and monetisation

● Ability to influence senior stakeholders and align multiple teams

● Marketplace, SaaS, or growth-heavy product experience preferred


Location: HSR layout in Bangalore

items.description: Join our India Tech Hub – Be among the first hires! Kobie, a 35-year veteran of the loyalty industry, a multi-year Forrester Leader, and USA Top Workplace is expanding its global footprint by establishing a Tech Hub in India. Kobie partners with global brands to build deep connections with their customers through personalized, data-driven loyalty experiences and has a mission of growing enterprise value through loyalty. The Tech Hub will serve as a Global Capabilities Center for a broad range of technology roles, and this is your chance to play a pivotal role in shaping our presence in India. Join us as we continue to lead in loyalty, delivering innovative customer experiences for some of the world’s most recognized brands while working alongside some of the best and brightest in loyalty.The Product Owner team makes Kobie's strategic product vision a reality through the development of technology solutions. We drive projects forward, collaborating with development teams and stakeholders to deliver unparalleled personalized experiences to customers worldwide. We are quick on our feet, using critical thinking and creative problem solving to optimize the value of products released. We champion platform standards to achieve long-lasting, quality outcomes that help our clients grow enterprise value through loyalty.How You Will Make An ImpactLead design and execution of roadmap enhancementsto Kobie Alchemy® Loyalty Cloud's loyalty decisioning (Experience Engine) and experience delivery (Producer) technology product suites. Gather requirements across clients and industry verticals to identify scope and maximize reusability of new feature requests. Manage the product backlogincluding dependencies, priority, sequence, strategy, and business objectives. Identify and address challenges that arise during the product development lifecycle, collaborating with team members to find creative solutions and keep development on track. Provide mechanisms for teams and stakeholders to view and track team and product work progress across releases and major product milestones. Share vision and purpose for all product development efforts, gaininginterest and buy-in from various audiences, including business and technical roles at multiple levels of the organization. Be a steward for excellence in your domain of expertise, advocating for and evolving platform standards while building the team's shared understanding. What You Need To Be Successful7+ years working in a Product Owner, Business Systems Analyst, Product Manager, or similar role Demonstrated experience driving Agile/Scrum delivery projects that involve web-based software development and 3rd party integrations via API and batch Strong technical background, with a knack for translating concepts into language business and tech roles can understand Dot connector, ability to identify patterns and navigate complex challenges Loyalty product, program, or industry experience a plus Familiarity with UX research and best practices a plus PMI-PBA/CBAP/CSPO/PSPO certification a plus  Bachelor's degree About KobieNamed a Top Workplace in the USA and Top Remote Workplace, Kobie is where the best minds in loyalty come together, driven by passion and innovation. We’re always looking for talented individuals ready to join a collaborative, growth-focused culture.As a trusted partner to some of the world’s most recognized brands, we are loyalty leaders, helping brands build lasting emotional connections with their consumers.  We do this with a strategy-led technology approach that uncovers the truth behind what drives consumers on an emotional level.As we launch our India Tech Hub, we are excited to bring our award-winning culture to a new region - creating an environment where collaboration, flexibility, and career growth come together to build something truly special.We are proud to be the only loyalty provider to be externally recognized for their culture. We believe people thrive when they feel valued, supported, and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion ensures every teammate has a voice and the opportunity to be heard.Giving back is in our DNA at Kobie,through an annual fundraiser, charitable partnerships, and volunteer opportunities, we encourage our teammates to make a difference in their communities.To support our teammates beyond just their careers, we offer highly competitive benefits, comprehensive health coverage, and well-being perks that support our teammates and their dependents. We understand the importance of time for life outside of work - recognizing public holidays,offering flexible time off, and prioritizing work-life balance.As we expand into India, our new teammates will be fully integrated with our U.S. teams, working on global projects and gaining exposure to top industry leaders. With continued growth, we will establish a physical office in Bengaluru, India, giving teammates aspace for collaboration and fostering connection.Now is the perfect time to join Kobie. Be part of something big and help shape the future of our global capabilities center, the Kobie India Tech Hub.We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
items.descriptionHtml:
Join our India Tech Hub – Be among the first hires!

Kobie, a 35-year veteran of the loyalty industry, a multi-year Forrester Leader, and USA Top Workplace is expanding its global footprint by establishing a Tech Hub in India. Kobie partners with global brands to build deep connections with their customers through personalized, data-driven loyalty experiences and has a mission of growing enterprise value through loyalty. The Tech Hub will serve as a Global Capabilities Center for a broad range of technology roles, and this is your chance to play a pivotal role in shaping our presence in India. Join us as we continue to lead in loyalty, delivering innovative customer experiences for some of the world’s most recognized brands while working alongside some of the best and brightest in loyalty.

The Product Owner team makes Kobie's strategic product vision a reality through the development of technology solutions. We drive projects forward, collaborating with development teams and stakeholders to deliver unparalleled personalized experiences to customers worldwide. We are quick on our feet, using critical thinking and creative problem solving to optimize the value of products released. We champion platform standards to achieve long-lasting, quality outcomes that help our clients grow enterprise value through loyalty.

How You Will Make An Impact

  • Lead design and execution of roadmap enhancementsto Kobie Alchemy® Loyalty Cloud's loyalty decisioning (Experience Engine) and experience delivery (Producer) technology product suites.
  • Gather requirements across clients and industry verticals to identify scope and maximize reusability of new feature requests.
  • Manage the product backlogincluding dependencies, priority, sequence, strategy, and business objectives.
  • Identify and address challenges that arise during the product development lifecycle, collaborating with team members to find creative solutions and keep development on track.
  • Provide mechanisms for teams and stakeholders to view and track team and product work progress across releases and major product milestones.
  • Share vision and purpose for all product development efforts, gaininginterest and buy-in from various audiences, including business and technical roles at multiple levels of the organization.
  • Be a steward for excellence in your domain of expertise, advocating for and evolving platform standards while building the team's shared understanding.

What You Need To Be Successful

  • 7+ years working in a Product Owner, Business Systems Analyst, Product Manager, or similar role
  • Demonstrated experience driving Agile/Scrum delivery projects that involve web-based software development and 3rd party integrations via API and batch
  • Strong technical background, with a knack for translating concepts into language business and tech roles can understand
  • Dot connector, ability to identify patterns and navigate complex challenges
  • Loyalty product, program, or industry experience a plus
  • Familiarity with UX research and best practices a plus
  • PMI-PBA/CBAP/CSPO/PSPO certification a plus 
  • Bachelor's degree

About Kobie

Named a Top Workplace in the USA and Top Remote Workplace, Kobie is where the best minds in loyalty come together, driven by passion and innovation. We’re always looking for talented individuals ready to join a collaborative, growth-focused culture.

As a trusted partner to some of the world’s most recognized brands, we are loyalty leaders, helping brands build lasting emotional connections with their consumers.  We do this with a strategy-led technology approach that uncovers the truth behind what drives consumers on an emotional level.As we launch our India Tech Hub, we are excited to bring our award-winning culture to a new region - creating an environment where collaboration, flexibility, and career growth come together to build something truly special.

We are proud to be the only loyalty provider to be externally recognized for their culture. We believe people thrive when they feel valued, supported, and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion ensures every teammate has a voice and the opportunity to be heard.

Giving back is in our DNA at Kobie,through an annual fundraiser, charitable partnerships, and volunteer opportunities, we encourage our teammates to make a difference in their communities.

To support our teammates beyond just their careers, we offer highly competitive benefits, comprehensive health coverage, and well-being perks that support our teammates and their dependents. We understand the importance of time for life outside of work - recognizing public holidays,offering flexible time off, and prioritizing work-life balance.

As we expand into India, our new teammates will be fully integrated with our U.S. teams, working on global projects and gaining exposure to top industry leaders. With continued growth, we will establish a physical office in Bengaluru, India, giving teammates aspace for collaboration and fostering connection.

Now is the perfect time to join Kobie. Be part of something big and help shape the future of our global capabilities center, the Kobie India Tech Hub.

These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Voyager (94001), India, Bangalore, Karnātaka

Manager, Product Management

We’re looking to hire a Manager, Product Management for AI/ML Model Governance to join the Machine Learning Experience (MLX) team!

At Capital One India, we work in a fast paced and intellectually rigorous environment to solve fundamental business problems at scale. Using advanced analytics, data science and machine learning, we derive valuable insights about product and process design, consumer behavior, regulatory and credit risk, and more from large volumes of data, and use it to build cutting edge patentable products that drive the business forward. Product Management at Capital One is a critical, vibrant craft that requires a blend of entrepreneurial drive, keen business acumen, customer experience obsession, technical depth and strong people leadership.

The MLX team is at the forefront of how Capital One builds and deploys responsible GenAI and ML models; we onboard and educate associates on the GenAI and ML platforms and products that the whole company uses; we drive new innovation and research; and we’re working to seamlessly infuse Generative AI and ML into the fabric of the company. The AI experience we're creating is the foundation upon which our lines of business will continue to deliver next-generation machine learning-driven products and services for our customers.

Specific responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

  • Develop and communicate the product vision and strategy for your area of responsibility
  • Deeply understand the needs of data scientists, machine learning engineers and model risk officers across Capital One
  • Partner with business and technology leadership to align on prioritization of key problems to be solved to maximize business and customer outcomes
  • Collaborate with teams in India and USA to drive alignment with different teams working on the platform
  • Incorporate design thinking and analytics to inform product design
  • Maintain a healthy backlog of work and play a critical role in many agile ceremonies.
  • Support the team with escalation and resolution of impediments
  • Serve as the connection between Customers, Capital One’s mission, and Tech. Partners

We want you if you are:

  • Interested in building lasting relationships with peers and having fun while building products that delight customers and drive value to the business.
  • You enjoy putting yourself in the shoes of customers to identify their pain points and then collaborating with cross-functional teams to solve these customer challenges. Even better if you have experience doing this for data scientists and machine learning engineers.
  • You are a learner who is able to quickly get up to speed in a highly technical environment.
  • Passionate about building a well-rounded product management skill set that is human centered, business focused, and technology driven with an emphasis on integrated problem solving and people leadership.
  • You are experienced working in an Agile environment.
  • You are a do-er who can also zoom out and think strategically.
  • You have excellent oral and written communication skills.
  • You are highly collaborative and have the ability to influence and lead.

Basic Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s in Engineering or Technology.
  • At least 8 years of work experience in tech industry
  • At least 5 years of experience in product management.
  • At least 3 years of experience in building Enterprise AI/ML platform products or AI/ML powered products for enterprises.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Expertise in building enterprise platform products with focus on AI/ML Model Governance, AI/ML Model Risk Management and Automation OR should have worked on LLM-powered co-pilot, agent assist capabilities.
  • Experience with Generative AI and Machine Learning technologies.
  • Experience in Data Science or Business Analytics.

At this time, Capital One will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

No agencies please. Capital One is an equal opportunity employer (EOE, including disability/vet) committed to non-discrimination in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. Capital One promotes a drug-free workplace. Capital One will consider for employment qualified applicants with a criminal history in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable laws regarding criminal background inquiries, including, to the extent applicable, Article 23-A of the New York Correction Law; San Francisco, California Police Code Article 49, Sections 4901-4920; New York City’s Fair Chance Act; Philadelphia’s Fair Criminal Records Screening Act; and other applicable federal, state, and local laws and regulations regarding criminal background inquiries.

If you have visited our website in search of information on employment opportunities or to apply for a position, and you require an accommodation, please contact Capital One Recruiting at 1-800-304-9102 or via email at RecruitingAccommodation@capitalone.com. All information you provide will be kept confidential and will be used only to the extent required to provide needed reasonable accommodations.

items.descriptionHtml:
Job Title

Product Manager–Fleet Management Solutions

Job Description

Product Manager–Fleet Management Solutions

The Product Manager – Fleet Management Solutions is accountable for defining and driving the end‑to‑end (E2E) fleet portfolio strategy, execution, and lifecycle management, translating customer needs, installed base insights, and business objectives into scalable digital and service solutions.

The role owns the definition and execution of integrated fleet, software, service, and lifecycle roadmaps (e.g., Protocol Manager, TechMax, CollabLive, subscription offerings), ensuring strong alignment with portfolio strategy, optimal performance, quality‑cost balance, and commercial success.

Key Objectives

  • Define and execute a clear fleet management portfolio strategy and roadmap
  • Deliver integrated solutions across the installed base and strengthen NPI proposition
  • Drive customer productivity, lifecycle value, and recurring revenue growth
  • Strengthen E2E ownership, value proposition clarity, and adoption performance

Your Role

End-to-End Portfolio Ownership & Cross BUs Alignment

  • Owns end‑to‑end (E2E) lifecycle management of the Fleet Management portfolio across equipment, software, and service domains
  • Ensures strong alignment across Business Units (BU), to deliver integrated and scalable solutions
  • Drives portfolio prioritization and trade-offs, balancing customer needs, regional demands, technical feasibility, and business impact
  • Acts as the single point of accountability for fleet portfolio performance and evolution across the organization

Strategy, Roadmap & Product Definition

  • Defines and drives the fleet portfolio strategy and roadmap, integrating:
    • Installed base insights (performance, lifecycle stage, ESL)
    • Customer workflows and productivity needs
    • Market trends, competitive landscape, and digital health evolution
  • Owns end-to-end product definition, ensuring clear requirements across software, service, and data-driven solutions
  • Aligns strategy with portfolio-level objectives and cross-segment dependencies
Value Proposition, Product Development & Lifecycle Execution

  • Owns and governs end‑to‑end value creation and lifecycle execution of the fleet portfolio: AD/ PDLM/MLD, VPC, Launch Brief, Launch Plan, Business Case, Claims, Customer Life Cycle Plan
  • Proactively evaluate customer feedback, market insights, and competitive dynamics and benchmark
  • Cross-functional leadership and alignment to secure pre-defined commercial (OIT, revenue, margin, penetration), user needs, quality and cost KPIs.

Go-to-Market & Adoption

  • Support Product Marketing and regions to:
    • Define and execute go-to-market strategies and plan
    • Enable successful product launches and adoption
  • Ensures clear and differentiated value proposition articulation
  • Monitors and addresses adoption gaps, driving corrective actions to ensure sustained market success
Stakeholder & Ecosystem Leadership

  • Builds and manages strong relationships across:
    • Regions, service organizations, and sales teams
    • Key customers and strategic partners
  • Acts as a subject-matter expert for fleet and lifecycle solutions
  • Provides strategic guidance, training, and enablement to support commercial success
You Are Fit If

  • 5+ years product management, finance, or business management experience
  • Experience in SaaS, digital platforms, or service solutions
  • Strong stakeholder management and analytical skills
  • Commercial acumen and data-driven decision-making
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills
  • Experience in healthcare, imaging, or IoT platforms will be preferred
  • Understanding of installed base lifecycle and subscription models will be preferred.

How We Work Together

We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.

Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.

Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.

This role is an office-based role

About Philips

We are a health technology company. We built our entire company around the belief that every human matters, and we won't stop until everybody everywhere has access to the quality healthcare that we all deserve. Do the work of your life to help the lives of others.

  • Learn more about our business.
  • Discover our rich and exciting history.
  • Learn more about our purpose.

If you’re interested in this role and have many, but not all, of the experiences needed, we encourage you to apply. You may still be the right candidate for this or other opportunities at Philips. Learn more about our culture of impact with care here.

items.descriptionHtml:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.

Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.

Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.

Job Description

  • Looking for a company that inspires passion, courage, and creativity, where you can be on the team shaping the future of global commerce? Want to shape how millions of people buy, sell, connect, and share around the world? If you’re interested in joining a purpose driven community that is dedicated to crafting an ambitious and inclusive work environment, join eBay – a company you can be proud to be with.
  • We’re on a mission to build the most intelligent and scalable product knowledge system in eCommerce, powered by AI, structured data, and next-gen taxonomy/ontology strategies. We’re looking for a Group Product Manager who thrives at the intersection of structured data, AI/ML, and search optimization—someone who can define the strategy, drive execution, and collaborate deeply with AI, data, and engineering teams to improve how millions of customers discover products. In this high-agency, startup-like role, you’ll own and scale the AI-driven product knowledge ecosystem, ensuring our taxonomy, ontology, and catalog data power better search, recommendations, and personalization experiences!


We are seeking a Group Product Manager, who is passionate about data, search, recommendation, online commerce, AI/ML, generative AI, LLMs, and buyer experience, to lead the Product team. The candidate should be comfortable dealing with ambiguity and have experience defining requirements, driving cross team collaboration, and communicating with executives. In this role, you will work with product managers, engineers, applied researchers, user researchers, designers, and analytics teams to define solutions and make data-driven decisions. This person will be responsible for leveraging generative AI technology to revolutionize Product Knowledge based application cutting across Search, Recommendations and Personalized experiences for eBay to ensure the success of our AI-based features.

Responsibilities

  • Define, implement & own the Product Strategy and Roadmap for products powered by generative AI that will cut across the Product Knowledge organization powering applications across the e-commerce application stack - from retrieval to ads to experiences
  • Translate product strategy into clear and concise specifications, make functional and user interface design tradeoffs, handle product launches, and lead the product successes post-launch.
  • Data driven product decisions with a balance of near-term deliverables and longer-term investment in technology platforms
  • Communicate strategies and trade-offs up to senior leaders and down to development teams
  • Put the customer first, understand market specific nuances, and identify algo improvements to address unique user & application specific needs
  • Drive collaboration across multiple teams to ensure teams are aligned and are working towards solving customer problems
  • Drive A/B testing to identify launch candidates that improve organizational goals
  • Define key metrics and evaluation frameworks to track the impact of structured data improvements on search, recommendations, and personalization.
  • Deeply Integrate with AI/ML Teams – Work hand-in-hand with AI, data science, and engineering to build scalable, automated solutions for listing classification and aspect intelligence and listing annotation


Preferred Qualifications

  • 10+ years with a strong track record product management experience, having previous experience building customer centric products
  • 6+ years of experience in AI/ML product management with specific emphasis on knowledge graphs, a 2+ of years of experience in GenAI and using LLMs to drive business outcomes
  • Strong understanding of product classification, metadata structuring, and entity resolution techniques.
  • Experience optimizing product search, recommendations, and structured navigation.
  • Technical Comfort – Familiarity with SQL, APIs, data pipelines, and AI/ML workflows.
  • A proven track record building new, innovative, and engaging customer experiences informed by customer data from various sources (market research, A/B testing, user research)
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to use data to back up assumptions, recommendations, and drive decision making
  • Technically savvy (prefer a technical role in past, or Computer Science Degree)
  • Passionate advocate for user
  • Demonstrated leadership ability, self-motivated and directed, entrepreneurial ability to innovate quickly
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills


Additional Details

eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.

We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

items.descriptionHtml:
About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and driven Product Owner to support the growth and execution of our Rich Communication Services (RCS) offering within our CPaaS product portfolio

You will work closely with the Product Manager, engineering, design, and delivery teams to translate business and market needs into actionable product requirements. Your role is central to ensuring that our RCS roadmap is implemented efficiently, with high quality, and in alignment with the global product strategy.


Key Responsibilities:


Product Execution & Delivery:

Translate product vision and high-level requirements into detailed user stories, acceptance criteria, and sprint backlogs.


Work with engineering teams to ensure clarity, feasibility, and timely delivery of product increments.


Manage the RCS product backlog, ensuring it is well-prioritized, refined, and aligned with business outcomes.


Participate in sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives to ensure continuous alignment between technical teams and product goals.


Validate deliverables against business and technical acceptance criteria to ensure high-quality releases.


Stakeholder Collaboration:


Serve as the day-to-day point of contact between engineering, QA, and cross-functional business teams.


Collaborate closely with the Product Manager to communicate progress, risks, and dependencies.


Support pre-sales and operations teams with product knowledge and technical clarifications when needed.

Ensure customer, carrier, and partner feedback is incorporated into product iterations.


Market & Technical Understanding:


Develop a solid understanding of RCS standards, GSMA specifications, and carrier-level

integrations.


Stay informed about RCS trends, API developments, and platform capabilities to support informed product decisions.


Contribute to use case validation and functional design to ensure product-market fit.


Data & Continuous Improvement:


Monitor product performance metrics post-launch (e.g., delivery success rates, engagement, adoption).

Identify opportunities to improve user experience, efficiency, or technical reliability.

Support documentation, release notes, and internal enablement material for RCS product updates.


Qualifications

3-5 years of experience as a Product Owner, Business Analyst, or Technical Product Lead, ideally within CPaaS, telecom or messaging platforms.


Strong familiarity with RCS, SMS, or other messaging APIs.


Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments, with hands-on backlog and sprint management.


Excellent ability to translate complex requirements into clear, testable user stories.


Solid communication and collaboration skills across technical and business teams.

Analytical mindset with a focus on product quality, delivery, and customer impact.

Bachelor s degree in engineering, computer science, or related field; Agile certification (CSPO, PSPO) preferred.


Preferred Attributes

Experience collaborating with mobile operators,


OEMs, or messaging aggregators.

Knowledge of GSMA RCS Universal Profile and API specifications.

Familiarity with API-first development, postman collections, and JSON workflows.

Strong attention to detail, organization, and proactive ownership mindset.

Interest in emerging communication technologies and their business applications.


Success in This Role Looks Like

The RCS product backlog is always clear, prioritized, and ready for execution.

Sprint deliverables consistently align with product vision and market expectations.

Engineering teams have clarity and support to execute efficiently.

High-quality, timely releases that drive adoption and customer satisfaction.

items.descriptionHtml:
Job Description

We are seeking an enthusiastic and detail-oriented Product Owner to join our dynamic team in Bengaluru, India. As a Product Owner, you will play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between our product vision and development execution.

The Product Owner will lead the development of fraud detection and prevention products within a FinTech environment. This role focuses on maximizing product value by defining and prioritizing features that reduce financial crime, improve customer trust, and meet regulatory requirements. You will work closely with engineering, data science, risk, and compliance teams to deliver scalable, real-time fraud solutions.

This is an excellent opportunity for a visionary professional who thrives in an agile environment and is passionate about building products that make a real impact.

  • Partner with Product Managers to develop, maintain, and communicate a clear and compelling product roadmap aligned with strategic business objectives
  • Create and prioritize a healthy, well-organized product backlog, ensuring all features and user stories are clearly defined with acceptance criteria
  • Write comprehensive user stories and conduct detailed requirements analysis to translate complex business needs into actionable development tasks
  • Define sprint goals and collaborate with development teams during planning activities to ensure clarity and alignment on deliverables
  • Conduct user journey mapping and usability testing to understand customer needs and validate product decisions with data-driven insights
  • Formally accept completed work and ensure it meets quality standards and acceptance criteria before release
  • Manage cross-team dependencies and communicate build progress, forecasted delivery timelines, and product performance to stakeholders
  • Support product discovery activities through elaboration of features from the roadmap, conducting ROI analysis to determine approval for build
  • Ensure Non-Functional Requirements are created, maintained, and tested against throughout the product lifecycle
  • Actively participate in agile ceremonies and scaled agile events, promoting agile values and driving continuous improvement through inspect and adapt cycles
  • Leverage business systems knowledge, industry requirements, and practical implications to support informed product priorities
  • Analyze product performance metrics and KPIs to recommend data-driven solutions and approaches to the Product Manager

  • Own the product vision and roadmap for fraud detection and prevention capabilities

  • Translate business, risk, and regulatory requirements into clear user stories and acceptance criteria

  • Manage and prioritize the product backlog based on fraud risk, customer impact, and ROI

  • Collaborate with data science teams on fraud models, rules engines, and machine-learning initiatives

  • Work closely with engineering teams to deliver real-time and batch fraud solutions

    Partner with Risk, Compliance, AML, and Operations teams to ensure regulatory alignment

  • Define and monitor KPIs such as fraud loss reduction, false positives, approval rates, and customer friction

  • Support model lifecycle management (feature changes, monitoring, retraining, explainability)

  • Ensure solutions comply with regulatory and data privacy requirements (e.g., PSD2, GDPR, PCI DSS)





Qualifications

Education:


  • Bachelor's degree in any discipline, preferably in Business, Information Technology, or related field

Required Experience:


  • Proven experience in a Product Owner or similar product management role
  • Demonstrated experience working within a software or product development organization
  • Proven track record of successfully interfacing and collaborating with cross-functional teams including development, design, and business stakeholders
  • Experience working in an agile or scaled agile environment

Essential Skills & Knowledge:


  • Proficiency in writing clear, concise user stories with well-defined acceptance criteria
  • Expert-level backlog prioritization and management skills
  • Solid understanding of user journey mapping and process mapping techniques
  • Experience conducting or interpreting usability testing and user feedback
  • Comprehensive knowledge of agile ways of working, agile metrics, and sprint execution
  • Strong understanding of compliance, governance stewardship, and regulatory requirements
  • Ability to define and track success metrics and OKRs
  • Proficiency with work management tools such as JIRA, Azure DevOps (ADO), or similar platforms
  • Hands-on experience with collaboration tools such as Miro or equivalent
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with a data-driven mindset
  • Demonstrated stakeholder management and relationship-building capabilities

  • Proven experience as a Product Owner or Product Manager in FinTech or financial services

  • Hands-on experience with fraud detection and prevention systems

  • Strong understanding of card, payment, or digital fraud (e.g., transaction fraud, account takeover, identity fraud)

  • Experience working with data-driven products and analytics platforms

  • Solid knowledge of Agile/Scrum frameworks

  • Ability to work with technical and non-technical stakeholders

  • Strong prioritization and decision-making skills in high-risk environments

Preferred Skills & Knowledge:


  • Experience with user research methodologies and user behavior analysis
  • Knowledge of user feedback analysis and incorporation into product decisions
  • Understanding of product strategy and strategic planning
  • Familiarity with agile metrics tools and dashboards
  • Experience in financial services or fintech organizations
  • Certification in product management or agile methodologies (Scrum, SAFe, etc.)

Behavioral Attributes:


  • Demonstrates exceptional organizational and time management skills
  • Shows strong attention to detail and commitment to quality
  • Exhibits collaborative and inclusive approach to working with diverse teams
  • Displays innovative thinking and openness to new ideas and approaches
  • Maintains transparency and integrity in all communications and decision-making
  • Demonstrates resilience and flexibility in dynamic, fast-paced environments
  • Shows genuine customer focus and empathy for end-user needs
  • Exhibits goal-oriented mindset with strong drive to deliver results
  • Maintains a positive, enthusiastic attitude even in challenging circumstances
  • Demonstrates decisiveness and ability to make informed decisions under uncertainty
  • Shows willingness to accept feedback constructively and adapt accordingly
  • Actively supports and celebrates the success of team members

Additional Information

At Endava, we’re committed to creating an open, inclusive, and respectful environment where everyone feels safe, valued, and empowered to be their best. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives—because we know that inclusive teams help us deliver smarter, more innovative solutions for our customers. Hiring decisions are based on merit, skills, qualifications, and potential. If you need adjustments or support during the recruitment process, please let us know.
