Job Description

Has accountability for the performance and results of a team within own discipline or function

Is recognised as an expert in own area within the organisation and has specialised depth and/or breadth of expertise in own discipline or function

Interprets internal or external issues and recommends solutions/best practices



Qualifications

Job Description

Job Role

Product Owner - IC4

Location



Reporting to

Product Owner Manager

Business Unit

Product

Level

F

Date



Brief Job Summary

The Product Owner works closely with the Product Manager to understand and deliver the strategic roadmap for the product. They will work with the development team and supporting roles to break that roadmap down into prioritised requirements. Working with support from the Product Manager they will ensure that the development team has a clear understanding of the product/platform vision and goals. They oversee product build progress, answering queries and helping solve impediments to the delivery. The role is part of our squad structure and can be aligned to any product or platform in SS, RS or Common Services.

Roles and Responsibilities

  • Support the Product Manager with the creation and update of a centrally mastered Product Roadmap.
  • Ensure the Product Roadmap is reflected correctly in our work management tooling (JIRA or ADO).
  • Creation of features and user stories with support from other roles in the form of a healthy prioritised backlog.
  • Definition of sprint goals to help focus the teams during their increment.
  • Formal acceptance of work as done and ready for release.
  • Support cross team dependency management.
  • Input to both product and build reporting as required including financial reporting and budgeting.
  • Use data to recommend to the Product Manager best solutions and approaches.
  • Creation of technical user stories where required and ability to prioritise these.
  • Plays an active role in agile and scaled agile events, supporting agile values and helping promote the continual improvement cycle through inspect & adapt.
  • Contribute to the product vision, goals, success measures and roadmap for the product.
  • Applies some domain knowledge or demonstrates ability to quickly learn about the product(s), market and customer base.
  • Understand what KPIs are most valuable to the business ensuring these are reflected in the product delivery.
  • Support the Product Manager in Product Discovery through the elaboration of product features from the product roadmap ultimately reaching approval to build based on ROI.
  • Work with the supporting roles and internal/external stakeholders to further refine approved product features through detailed requirements analysis/design to get work ready for planning & build documented through user stories.
  • Ensure Non Functional Requirements are being created, maintained and tested against.
  • Work directly with Info Sec and or DPO on product change that need their approval to ensure the product is compliant
  • Support the development teams in planning activities at both the quarter and increment level where applicable.
  • Work with the development team and actively suggest improvements that can be made to how the team work in a collaborative way
  • Assimilate complex detailed problems and translate them into easily digestible content for cross-functional team members.
  • Accountable for the prioritisation of user stories through their lifecycle.
  • Ensure features & user stories remain aligned to the roadmap and strategic objectives set by the Product Manager.
  • Accountable for the prioritisation of user stories through their lifecycle
  • Leverage working knowledge of business systems, broader industry requirements, and practical implications of enhancements to support product priorities.
  • Support the update to stakeholders and other P&E teams on the build progression and forecasted delivery.
  • Support the update to stakeholders and other P&E teams on the product performance utilising metrics where possible.
  • Support the defiinition of the product practice. scope.
  • Play an active role in the product owner practice and help lead on some topics to assist with the upskilling of the wider team.

Job Specifications

Education

  • Any graduation preferably Business or IT

Relevant Experience

  • Experience working within a software or product development organization preferred although not required.
  • Experience working within a financial organization preferred although not required.
  • Product Ownership or similar role experience.
  • Proven experience of interfacing with cross-functional teams.



Functional Skills

Behavioural Skills

Knowledge and skills

Will have good understanding of:




Essential:




  • Business analysis
  • Writing user stories
  • Backlog prioritisation
  • Defining acceptance criteria
  • User journey & process mapping
  • Usability testing
  • Agile ways of working
  • Agile metrics
  • Compliance & Governance stewardship
  • Success metrics & OKRs
  • JIRA, ADO or similar.
  • Miro or similar collaboration tools.



Desirable:




  • User research
  • User feedback analysis
  • User behaviour analysis
  • Product strategy
  • Agile Metric tools.



  • Demonstrates high ethical standards and integrity in their actions and decision-making
  • Is recognised as calm, composed, polite and respectful in all situations and circumstances.
  • Practices and promotes openness and transparency.
  • Regularly self reflects and supports others within their squad to do the same.
  • Will be positive and enthusiastic, even in difficult circumstances.
  • Will be self-motivating and maintain a solution-focused attitude.
  • Will promote optimism and motivate others within their squad.
  • Celebrates the success of others.
  • Openly praises other members of their squad and publicly recognises success.
  • Champions their squad to others.
  • Accepts feedback easily without confrontation and responds to it, acknowledging the perspective of others.
  • Highlights any impediments or risks constructively.
  • Provides positive and constructive opinions in conversations.
  • Helps to avoid conflict within their squad and other squads by spotting contentious matters and dealing with them.





















Role Grading

Role Grading Information

Job function knowledge

Measures the function-specific technical knowledge required to perform specific tasks associated with the job.

Please explain technical knowledge required to perform specific tasks associated with the job

  • Maintains an extensive understanding of emerging practices and their application and takes responsibility for driving own and team members’ development opportunities and new ways of working.
  • Shows determination to learn and showcase new function-specific technical skills, and pass on knowledge.

Business Expertise

Measures the extent of required knowledge beyond the job. It refers to both internal and external business expertise.

Please explain the extent of required knowledge beyond the job

  • Fully understands the departmental domain, strategies, practices and processes
  • Quickly understands new information and applies if effectively.
  • Drives opportunities for others to learn from their expertise and experience.
  • Seeks opportunities to transfer any unique knowledge they may possess to others.
  • Fully understands who the wider customers or stakeholders are and shares with others.
  • Has good awareness of the commercial and business background of their work.
  • Drives discussions on the expectations of the business on them as individuals and their team.
  • Fully understands the requirements of the end customer.
  • Seeks feedback on the wider customer experience.

Leadership

Refers to the people management required for the job.

Does the job require any people management (if so please give details)?

  • Frequently leads and supports their team.

Problem-solving

Measures the degree of mental and analytical skills required to perform the job.

Please explain the degree of mental and analytical skills required to perform the job

  • Applies a methodical, analytical approach to investigating and resolving complex problems.
  • Escalates appriopriately when scope or deadlines are impacted.

Nature of impact

Measures how the job significantly impacts the business by measuring the overall responsibility associated with the job.

Please explain how the job significantly impacts the business

  • Complex domain or smaller program related activities that consist of multiple outcomes or outcomes or projects.

Area of impact

Specific organisational entities where the impact is felt (ie on which part of the organisation the tasks performed or the decisions made have a direct influence).

Please give detail of which specific entities are impacted by the role (the part of the organisation the tasks performed or the decisions made have a direct influence)

  • Product or Service, Processes, Team, Suppliers

Interpersonal skills

Depicts the level of people skills required to perform the job. (ie how the job holder needs to be able to interact with others).

Please explain the level of people skills required to perform the job

  • Demonstrates excellent oral and written communication skills when engaging with colleagues and suppliers.
  • Proactively encourages others to explore new ideas and drives the ongoing development of them.
  • Works with teams, stakeholders and suppliers, seeking interaction with others in the context of their work.
  • Plays an active role amongst teams and is accountable for the performance and results of their team.
  • Is an active contributor in team discussions.
  • Consults all relevant stakeholders before decisions are made to ensure that their input has been considered.
  • Aware of their own, their teams and other teams' strengths and limitations and drives behaviour to address the limitations.

Additional Information

At Endava, we’re committed to creating an open, inclusive, and respectful environment where everyone feels safe, valued, and empowered to be their best. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives—because we know that inclusive teams help us deliver smarter, more innovative solutions for our customers. Hiring decisions are based on merit, skills, qualifications, and potential. If you need adjustments or support during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Brief Job Summary

The Product Owner works closely with the Product Manager to understand and deliver the strategic roadmap for the product. They will work with the development team and supporting roles to break that roadmap down into prioritised requirements. Working with support from the Product Manager they will ensure that the development team has a clear understanding of the product/platform vision and goals. They oversee product build progress, answering queries and helping solve impediments to the delivery. The role is part of our squad structure and can be aligned to any product or platform in SS, RS or Common Services.

Roles and Responsibilities

  • Support the Product Manager with the creation and update of a centrally mastered Product Roadmap.
  • Ensure the Product Roadmap is reflected correctly in our work management tooling (JIRA or ADO).
  • Creation of features and user stories with support from other roles in the form of a healthy prioritised backlog.
  • Definition of sprint goals to help focus the teams during their increment.
  • Formal acceptance of work as done and ready for release.
  • Support cross team dependency management.
  • Input to both product and build reporting as required including financial reporting and budgeting.
  • Use data to recommend to the Product Manager best solutions and approaches.
  • Creation of technical user stories where required and ability to prioritise these.
  • Plays an active role in agile and scaled agile events, supporting agile values and helping promote the continual improvement cycle through inspect & adapt.
  • Contribute to the product vision, goals, success measures and roadmap for the product.

  • Support the Product Manager in Product Discovery through the elaboration of product features from the product roadmap ultimately reaching approval to build based on ROI.
  • Work with the supporting roles and internal/external stakeholders to further refine approved product features through detailed requirements analysis/design to get work ready for planning & build documented through user stories.
  • Ensure Non Functional Requirements are being created, maintained and tested against.

  • Accountable for the prioritisation of user stories through their lifecycle

items.descriptionHtml:
We are looking to hire a Manager, Product Management for AI/ML Developer Experiences to join our team. This role will concentrate on enhancing the Developer Experience by creating GenAI-powered co-pilots and agents.


At Capital One India, we operate in a dynamic and intellectually stimulating environment, tackling fundamental business challenges at scale. We leverage advanced analytics, data science, and machine learning to extract valuable insights from vast datasets, informing product and process design, consumer behavior, regulatory and credit risk, and more. These insights are then used to develop cutting-edge, patentable products that propel the business forward. Product Management at Capital One is a vital and vibrant craft, demanding a blend of entrepreneurial drive, sharp business acumen, an obsession with customer experience, technical depth, and strong people leadership.


The MLX team is at the forefront of how Capital One builds and deploys responsible GenAI and ML models. We onboard and educate associates on the GenAI and ML platforms and products used across the company, drive new innovation and research, and seamlessly infuse Generative AI and ML into the fabric of the organization. The AI experience we're creating forms the foundation upon which our lines of business will continue to deliver next-generation machine learning-driven products and services for our customers.


Specific responsibilities include, but are not limited to:


  • Develop and communicate the product vision and strategy for your area of responsibility, with a focus on building GenAI powered co-pilots and agents
  • Deeply understand the needs of the developer community, support teams, business customers, data scientists, machine learning engineers, and model risk officers across Capital One.
  • Partner with business and technology leadership to align on prioritization of key problems to be solved to maximize business and customer outcomes.
  • Collaborate with teams in India and USA to drive alignment with different teams working on the platform.
  • Incorporate design thinking and analytics to inform product design, with a strong emphasis on customer-centricity.
  • Maintain a healthy backlog of work and play a critical role in agile ceremonies.
  • Support the team with escalation and resolution of impediments.
  • Serve as the connection between Customers, Capital One’s mission, and Tech Partners.
  • Collaborate with the design team and guide their investments for research, prototyping, experimentation, and overall design thinking.
  • Clearly communicate product plans, benefits, and results, as appropriate, to a spectrum of audiences, from internal stakeholders to executives, employees to end-users.


We want you if you are:

  • Interested in building lasting relationships with peers and having fun while building products that delight customers and drive value to the business.
  • Enjoy putting yourself in the shoes of customers to identify their pain points and then collaborating with cross-functional teams to solve these customer challenges. Even better if you have experience doing this for data scientists and machine learning engineers.
  • Learner who is able to quickly get up to speed in a highly technical environment.
  • Passionate about building a well-rounded product management skill set that is human-centered, business-focused, and technology-driven with an emphasis on integrated problem solving and people leadership.
  • Experienced working in an Agile environment.
  • A do-er who can also zoom out and think strategically.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills.
  • Highly collaborative and have the ability to influence and lead.


Basic Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s in Engineering or Technology.
  • At least 8 years of experience.
  • At least 5 years of experience in product management.
  • At least 3 years of experience in building AI-powered products and AI platform products for enterprises.
  • Product mindset and customer-centric approach to building, experimenting, and iterating products.


Preferred Qualifications:

  • MBA or Master’s degree.
  • 4+ years of experience with Generative AI, AI and Machine Learning technologies.
  • 2+ years of experience in Data Science or Business Analytics.
  • Experience working with LLM-powered co-pilot, agent assist capabilities.


At this time, Capital One will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

items.descriptionHtml:
Description

Upstream Defect Elimination (UDE) is a Senior Leadership Team mission to remove unnecessary costs and invent growth opportunities in Amazon's retail business. UDE requires a myriad of software and AI mechanisms. We're in search of a Sr. Product Manager to lead our human in the loop defect elimination artificial intelligence platform.

Retail Business Services (RBS) is the force multiplier team for Amazon’s Retail business growth worldwide through three core strategic initiatives: (1) Defect Elimination - identifying retail defects at their root cause and driving extirpation to increase margins; (2) Selection - enriching Amazon inventory data increasing gross sales; and, (3) Operational Excellence - to ensure Amazon's retail business is running efficiently and effectively.

The RBS Cost to Serve team works on an array of technologies ranging from automation that leverages Machine/Deep Learning to scale execution of highly complex tasks to scaled out platform software. We're in beginning stages of building a horizontal internal platform complete with a front end interface to oversee our UDE program; and, we need an experienced Sr. Product Manager to lead product development.

As the Defect Elimination AI Platform Sr. Product Manager, you would manage the development life cycle of our nascent technology in partnership with both a Product Leader and Engineering team. The day-to-day responsibilities include, but are not limited authoring requirements documents; hands-on system validation; unblocking Dev team issues; ensuring teams are tracking to committed timelines; developing a road map; aligning with stakeholders; collaborating with partner teams; and ensuring a successful, on-time launch.

Work/Life Balance

RBS puts a high value on work-life harmony. We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and we encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

Mentorship & Career Growth

Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. Also, we provide trainings to employees through online learning platforms and encourage employees to take up AWS/ML certifications.

Key job responsibilities

  • Experience of managing complex business processes at scale.
  • Drive end-to-end high impact and high visibility programs that impact WW Retail and hence customer experience on Amazon
  • Communication and influencing skills with an ability to deal with a fast-paced environment.
  • Experience in defining, developing and using metrics to drive the vision and manage the business.
  • Analyze data and dive deep into processes to derive insights for enhancing systems
  • Ability to lead not only at the strategic level, but also tactically day-to-day by diving deeply into business and technical domains.
  • Liaise with tech, product, ops and category teams to ensure timely deliverables for key projects
  • Ability to work effectively & independently in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines.
  • Write compelling business documents and drive outcomes for senior leadership at Amazon

A day in the life

  • Collaborating with cross-team stakeholders
  • Extracting requirements and authoring technical feature documents
  • Aligning with Engineering counterparts to ensure development is done to specification
  • Driving meetings to impactful conclusions
  • Product advocacy communications
  • Daily adherence to Amazon's Leadership Principles

About The Team

Retail Business Services (RBS) supports Amazon’s Retail business growth WW through three core tasks. These are (a) Selection, where RBS sources, creates and enrich ASINs to drive GMS growth; (b) Defect Elimination: where RBS resolves inbound supply chain defects and develops root cause fixes to improve free cash flow and (c) supports operational process for WW Retail teams where there is an air gap in the tech stack. The tech team in RBS develops automation that leverages Machine/Deep Learning to scale execution of these high complex tasks that currently require human cognitive skills.

Basic Qualifications

  • 5+ years of product or program management, product marketing, business development or technology experience
  • Experience with end to end product delivery
  • Experience with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a product
  • Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition
  • Experience as a product manager or owner
  • Experience owning technology products

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in influencing senior leadership through data driven insights
  • Experience working across functional teams and senior stakeholders
  • Experience in delivering end-to-end customer research studies, including study design, fieldwork (qualitative research moderation and survey execution), analysis, recommendations, reporting and presentation of results to senior leadership

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

items.descriptionHtml:
About WorkSpan

The next era of growth is being driven by business interoperability. Cloud, genAI, solutions combining services and software- more and more, companies outpace their competition not just through building superior products, but by creating stronger partnerships, paths to market, and better business models for winning together. Cloud providers, service providers, tech partners and resellers are teaming up to win more deals together through co-selling.

WorkSpan is building the world’s largest, trusted co-selling network.

WorkSpan already has seven of the world’s ten largest partner ecosystems on our platform and $50B of customer pipeline under active management. AWS, Google, Microsoft, MongoDB, PagerDuty, Databricks and dozens of others trust WorkSpan to accelerate and amplify their ecosystem strategies.

With a $30M series C and backing from world class investors Insight Partners, Mayfield, and M12, WorkSpan is poised to drive the future of B2B. Come be a part of it.

Join Our Team For The Opportunity To

  • Own your results and make a tangible impact on the business
  • Develop a deep understanding of GTM working closely with leadership across sales & marketing
  • Work with driven, passionate people every day
  • Be a part of an ambitious, supportive team on a mission

The Role: Principal Product Manager, Growth

As the GPM / Principal Product Manager for Growth, you will be the driving force behind WorkSpan’s evolution into an AI-native growth engine. You will sit at the intersection of Product, Data, and GTM strategy, owning the end-to-end user journey for our agentic AI platform.

This is a unique "full-stack" product role where you will be both the strategic architect—deciding between PLG and Product-Led Sales (PLS) motions—and the operational lead driving execution. Beyond optimizing the funnel, you will own specific product features and capabilities, evolving the product based on direct learnings from onboarding and scaling customers.

Your Primary Responsibilities

Growth Strategy & Experimentation

  • Define the Growth Motion: Strategically evaluate and implement both PLG (self-serve) and PLS (sales-assisted) motions to determine the most effective path for different market segments.
  • Conversion Optimization: Design and execute rapid experiments across the user lifecycle to drive acquisition, activation, and retention.
  • Monetization & Pricing: Develop strategies for in-product triggers, packaging, and self-service expansion to accelerate revenue growth.

Product Ownership & Capability Building

  • Feature Roadmap: Own key product features and core capabilities, ensuring they evolve to meet the needs of an AI-native ecosystem.
  • Onboarding to Value: Directly manage the product experience for initial onboarding, translating customer friction into high-impact product enhancements.
  • AI-Driven Growth: Leverage WorkSpan’s agentic AI capabilities to create "intelligent" growth loops and automated user engagement.

Operational Execution & Insights

  • Execution Leadership: Move seamlessly from high-level strategy to hands-on execution, collaborating with engineering and design to ship growth-focused updates.
  • Customer & Market Insights: Engage deeply with users to understand pain points, using these insights to validate positioning and product direction.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Marketing on demand generation and Sales leadership on deal strategies and PQL (Product Qualified Lead) handoffs.

Required Qualifications

  • 5+ years B2B SaaS experience, preferably in high-growth ecosystems and with focus on growing the MAU / DAU.
  • Deep Growth Expertise: Proven track record in Product-Led Growth (PLG) or Growth Product Management, with a focus on driving measurable revenue impact.
  • Strategic & Operational Hybrid: Ability to define long-term growth strategy while maintaining the "builder" mindset to drive daily execution.
  • AI/ML Familiarity: Experience or strong interest in AI-native product positioning and adoption strategies.
  • Exceptional Communication: Ability to craft compelling narratives and present complex product value to both technical and executive audiences.
  • Data-Driven Approach: Comfortable analyzing user metrics, win/loss data, and market trends to inform product decisions.
  • Technical Fluency: Ability to understand technical architectures and APIs well enough to articulate value and deliver demos.

What This Role Is NOT

  • Not a "Maintenance" PM role: You won't just be managing a legacy roadmap; you are responsible for inventing the growth engine.
  • Not just an Inbound role: You own the "what" and "how" of the growth features, but you must stay market-facing to ensure they resonate.

Company Perks & Benefits

  • Competitive salary, equity, and performance bonus
  • Unlimited vacation
  • Latest access to devices, learning and tools
  • Medical insurance
  • Monthly Wellness Stipend
  • Work from Office (Whitefield, Bengaluru)
  • Ability to work with people with experience in building and scaling startups and seen successful exits in the past
  • Have access to marquee clients - Nvidia, AMD, Anthropic, Cisco, SAP (to name a few)

items.descriptionHtml:
Description

Responsibilities:

Marketplace Strategy & Partnership Development:

  • Map the AI data landscape: Develop a comprehensive view of what data types frontier model builders need most and where market gaps exist that we can uniquely fill.
  • Integrate our existing capabilities: Orchestrate synthetic data pipelines, expert-curated data from GenAI business line, generalist annotations from search/ads workflows, and physical-world data from automotive pipelines into a unified marketplace offering.
  • Unlock new data sources: Forge partnerships to access premium content behind paywalls and enterprise datasets that haven't been available to AI builders.
  • Onboard external data producers: Identify organisations generating valuable datasets that model builders need but lack the infrastructure to monetise on their own.
  • Structure win-win deals: Design partnership models and monetisation frameworks that work for data producers, AI companies, and our platform.
  • Build the technical integration: Define how diverse data sources connect to our marketplace infrastructure while maintaining quality standards and provenance tracking.
  • Execute the vision: Transform fragmented data capabilities into a coherent marketplace that establishes TELUS Digital AI as the central hub where AI builders source training data.

Product Strategy & Roadmap Definition:

  • Define the product vision, strategy, and phased roadmap for the AI Data Marketplace-including data discovery, licensing models, quality verification, and integration capabilities.
  • Prioritise features ruthlessly based on marketplace impact: enabling seamless data discovery, streamlining partnership onboarding, building trust through provenance tracking, and creating transparent monetisation mechanisms.
  • Develop clear, actionable product requirements, user stories, and specifications that effectively bridge operational needs with technical solutions.

Execution & Cross-Functional Leadership:

  • Drive the product development lifecycle from ideation through requirements, design oversight, launch, and iteration.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Engineering, Business Development, Legal, Finance, and existing product lines to ensure marketplace velocity and strategic alignment.
  • Champion a data-driven approach, using marketplace metrics (supply/demand balance, deal velocity, data quality signals) to continuously refine product strategy and partnership models

Qualifications:

  • Proven experience (8-10 years) in Product Management, with a clear track record of launching and iterating on complex systems, operational tools, or data platforms.
  • Demonstrated ability and willingness to deeply understand complex operational workflows first-hand and translate those insights into impactful product requirements.
  • Prior experience in B2B, partnerships, or growth product management.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; highly proficient in using data (quantitative and qualitative) to diagnose problems and guide decisions.
  • Excellent cross-functional collaboration and communication skills – capable of building strong relationships and driving alignment across diverse teams (ops, technical, business).
  • Experience defining product vision, strategy, and roadmaps, particularly in ambiguous or rapidly evolving environments.

Preferred / Bonus:

  • Experience in domains directly related to data operations, data annotation platforms, data quality systems, AI/ML infrastructure, or managing specialised expert talent networks.
  • Past entrepreneurial experience, or as a software/ML engineer or designer
  • Experience working on multi-sided marketplaces
  • Experience with products designed for technical, scientific, or highly skilled knowledge workers.
  • You have multiple side projects/initiatives and the work to show for it

About Company:

TELUS Digital is the customer experience transformation partner to the world's most admired brands. Our diverse team weaves data, technology, and human ingenuity to deliver differentiated customer journeys, drive operational effectiveness, and scale AI solutions with meaningful value and positive impact.

We craft real-world solutions in the moments that matter, from customer acquisition to lifelong loyalty. Enabled by our global reach - spanning 78,000 experts in 33 countries - and deep industry expertise, we help over 600 organizations make the customer experience feel effortless.

Our solutions span Data & AI, Digital Experience & IT, CX Management and Trust & Safety. At the core of our innovation is Fuel iX™, an enterprise-grade generative AI platform that helps clients safely access and optimize leading LLMs to scale their own AI from pilot to production.

At TELUS Digital, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. All aspects of employment, including the decision to hire and promote, are based on applicant's qualifications, merits, competence and performance without regard to any characteristic related to diversity.

items.description: Company DescriptionRalph Lauren Corporation (NYSE:RL) is a global leader in the design, marketing and distribution of premium lifestyle products in five categories: apparel, accessories, home, fragrances, and hospitality. For more than 50 years, Ralph Lauren's reputation and distinctive image have been consistently developed across an expanding number of products, brands and international markets. The Company's brand names, which include Ralph Lauren, Ralph Lauren Collection, Ralph Lauren Purple Label, Polo Ralph Lauren, Double RL, Lauren Ralph Lauren, Polo Ralph Lauren Children, Chaps, among others, constitute one of the world's most widely recognized families of consumer brands. At Ralph Lauren, we unite and inspire the communities within our company as well as those in which we serve by amplifying voices and perspectives to create a culture of belonging, ensuring inclusion, and fairness for all. We foster a culture of inclusion through: Talent, Education & Communication, Employee Groups and Celebration.Position OverviewRalph Lauren is seeking a passionate Product Manager to drive the discovery, documentation, and execution of the functional and system requirements. This role will be responsible for understanding business needs and challenges and working with key stakeholders. This role will be an integral part of the digital team to communicate and refine requirements towards the goal of designing and delivering both technical as well as consumer-centric solutions. Experience with e-commerce, and in-store consumer experience is desired as this would benefit in delivering solutions that contribute to the success of the business.Essential Duties & Responsibilities Gather and document business and functional requirements by leveraging discovery sessions, requirement workshops, document analysis, use cases, user stories, and workflow analysis. Translate high-level information into digestible details and specifications, as well as abstract up from low-level information to a general understanding. Critically evaluate information from multiple sources in order to reconcile conflicts, align to clear goals, influence product direction, and drive solutions. Proactively communicate with external and internal customers to analyze the feasibility of new features and enhancement, understand information needs, as well as capture requirements. Work closely with a cross-functional, dynamic, and multi-disciplinary team of business, store associates, architects, developers, and subject matter experts to establish the technical vision, analyze tradeoffs, and actively mitigate potential risks. Determine operational performance via data analysis in order to measure success against goals and identify root causes of complex problems. Assist with the rollout of new functionality and user acceptance testing, ensuring that product stipulations are satisfied, issues are resolved, and exceptional user experiences are delivered. Ensure end users have the training and documentation required to efficiently manage new features. Partner with business teams in documentation of user manual and training document for users of the application Provide support and triage complex issues, outlining the impact and potential resolutions to stakeholders while working with the development team to implement the best solution. Assist with the rollout of new functionality and the execution of test plans to ensure that product stipulations are satisfied, issues are resolved, and exceptional user experiences are deliveredExperience, Skills & Knowledge Bachelor's degree, computer science or similar technology degree. Product management experience with a track record of delivering high quality results. Experience writing and vetting business requirement documentation, functional and system requirements, user stories, process workflows, and test cases is desired. Analytical and product development skills with a thorough understanding of how to interpret customer and business needs, translating them to application and operational requirements. Excellent communication/writing skills as well as the ability to work effectively on multiple projects simultaneously and across internal and external organizations. Comfortable in a highly-matrixed, iterative development environment and good at balancing collaboration and advocacy. Able to work independently as well as under direction of product and project leaders.

Company Description

Ralph Lauren Corporation (NYSE:RL) is a global leader in the design, marketing and distribution of premium lifestyle products in five categories: apparel, accessories, home, fragrances, and hospitality. For more than 50 years, Ralph Lauren's reputation and distinctive image have been consistently developed across an expanding number of products, brands and international markets. The Company's brand names, which include Ralph Lauren, Ralph Lauren Collection, Ralph Lauren Purple Label, Polo Ralph Lauren, Double RL, Lauren Ralph Lauren, Polo Ralph Lauren Children, Chaps, among others, constitute one of the world's most widely recognized families of consumer brands. At Ralph Lauren, we unite and inspire the communities within our company as well as those in which we serve by amplifying voices and perspectives to create a culture of belonging, ensuring inclusion, and fairness for all. We foster a culture of inclusion through: Talent, Education & Communication, Employee Groups and Celebration.

Position Overview

Ralph Lauren is seeking a passionate Product Manager to drive the discovery, documentation, and execution of the functional and system requirements. This role will be responsible for understanding business needs and challenges and working with key stakeholders. This role will be an integral part of the digital team to communicate and refine requirements towards the goal of designing and delivering both technical as well as consumer-centric solutions. Experience with e-commerce, and in-store consumer experience is desired as this would benefit in delivering solutions that contribute to the success of the business.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

  • Gather and document business and functional requirements by leveraging discovery sessions, requirement workshops, document analysis, use cases, user stories, and workflow analysis.
  • Translate high-level information into digestible details and specifications, as well as abstract up from low-level information to a general understanding.
  • Critically evaluate information from multiple sources in order to reconcile conflicts, align to clear goals, influence product direction, and drive solutions.
  • Proactively communicate with external and internal customers to analyze the feasibility of new features and enhancement, understand information needs, as well as capture requirements.
  • Work closely with a cross-functional, dynamic, and multi-disciplinary team of business, store associates, architects, developers, and subject matter experts to establish the technical vision, analyze tradeoffs, and actively mitigate potential risks.
  • Determine operational performance via data analysis in order to measure success against goals and identify root causes of complex problems.
  • Assist with the rollout of new functionality and user acceptance testing, ensuring that product stipulations are satisfied, issues are resolved, and exceptional user experiences are delivered.
  • Ensure end users have the training and documentation required to efficiently manage new features.
  • Partner with business teams in documentation of user manual and training document for users of the application
  • Provide support and triage complex issues, outlining the impact and potential resolutions to stakeholders while working with the development team to implement the best solution.
  • Assist with the rollout of new functionality and the execution of test plans to ensure that product stipulations are satisfied, issues are resolved, and exceptional user experiences are delivered

Experience, Skills & Knowledge

  • Bachelor's degree, computer science or similar technology degree.
  • Product management experience with a track record of delivering high quality results.
  • Experience writing and vetting business requirement documentation, functional and system requirements, user stories, process workflows, and test cases is desired.
  • Analytical and product development skills with a thorough understanding of how to interpret customer and business needs, translating them to application and operational requirements.
  • Excellent communication/writing skills as well as the ability to work effectively on multiple projects simultaneously and across internal and external organizations.
  • Comfortable in a highly-matrixed, iterative development environment and good at balancing collaboration and advocacy. Able to work independently as well as under direction of product and project leaders.

items.descriptionHtml:
About Us

Scapia! A credit card that’s out there to make travel happen for people, by converting their everyday expenses into travel experiences. We’re a bunch of passionate people who work together, brainstorm, and debate with each other, and don’t stop until we’re proud of our work. Customer delight tops everything else! We’ve worked hard to create an environment of honesty and passion that sets everyone up for success.


Role

We are looking for a Group Product Manager – Cards to lead the strategy, development, and growth of our credit card products issued in partnership with leading banks and NBFCs. This role will own the experience of customer acquisition and onboarding across multiple bank partners.

You will work closely with engineering, design, business, data, risk, compliance, and partner banks to build seamless and innovative credit card experiences for our users.


What you’ll do?

Product Strategy & Ownership

  • Define and execute the product strategy and roadmap for our credit card products.
  • Own the end-to-end card lifecycle, including onboarding, KYC flows, approval journeys, card activation, transactions, billing, and repayments.
  • Identify opportunities to improve card usage, engagement, and customer experience.
  • Build and coach a team of product managers who will work with you to ensure a fantastic experience for our customers.

Credit Card Ecosystem

  • Build and manage products across card networks like Visa, RuPay and enable users to get access to our cards.
  • Work closely with issuing bank partners to launch and scale co-branded credit card programs.
  • Manage integrations with partner banks, TSPs and infrastructure providers.

Product Execution

  • Translate business needs into clear PRDs, user stories, and product specifications.
  • Work with engineering and design to deliver high-quality product features and improvements.
  • Own product launches, experiments, and iterative improvements.

Growth & Engagement

  • Drive key metrics like conversion rate, card activation, monthly spend per user, and retention.
  • Build features that improve credit card onboarding, adoption and transaction frequency.
  • Partner with marketing and growth teams to launch rewards, offers, and engagement campaigns.

Data & Insights

  • Define product KPIs and track performance using data dashboards.
  • Use customer insights and analytics to improve product experiences and drive growth.

AI-Driven Innovation

  • Use AI tools to optimise workflows for yourselves and your team
  • Build intelligent features and internal tools


Profile

  • 9-12 years of product management experience, ideally in fintech, payments, or banking.
  • Understanding of credit card ecosystems, issuing banks, and payment networks is a plus.
  • Proven ability to ship customer-facing products at scale and own the process end-to-end.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a data-driven mindset.
  • Experience collaborating with engineering, design, business and external stakeholders.

items.title: Product Owner
items.sector: IT Services and IT Consulting and Software Development
items.description: Job Title: AI Solution Owner / Product Owner.Location: Sattva Magnificia, Old Madras Road, Bangalore.The InRhythm OpportunityInRhythm is establishing a modern software engineering practice built around an AI-driven Development Lifecycle (AI-DLC) methodology — spec-driven, agentic, and powered by Claude. As an AI Solution Owner, you will be the engine behind the pod’s delivery velocity, sitting at the intersection of business requirements and AI-augmented engineering. You will translate product intent into precisely structured specifications that Claude can act on with minimal ambiguity. This is a uniquely technical business role: part product owner, part prompt engineer, part delivery lead.Your ImpactThe velocity and accuracy of the entire AI-DLC pod depends on the quality of your specifications. Poorly structured requirements produce mediocre AI output. Your work directly determines how much rework engineers face, how fast features reach production, and how well the agentic pipeline scales as the practice grows. Every spec template, prompt pattern, and context pack you create will become reusable intellectual property for the firm.What You Will DoOwn the Spec Pipeline: Author, maintain, and continuously improve the structured feature specifications that Claude uses to generate code, covering backend APIs, data models, service contracts, and acceptance criteria.Design Spec Templates: Build and evolve reusable specification templates aligned to the Node.js/Java stack, ensuring they surface the context Claude needs including domain model, existing patterns, constraints, and edge cases.Manage the AI-DLC Workflow: Iterate on the end-to-end AI-DLC pipeline from spec ingestion through Claude code generation, human review, and merge governance.Maintain Codebase Context Packs: Own and update the architecture documents, coding convention guides, glossaries, and domain model summaries that are injected into Claude’s context window.Prioritize AI-Suitable Work: Assess the feature backlog for agentic suitability, identifying which features Claude can lead on, which require more human involvement, and why.Measure and Improve: Track agent output quality metrics including rework rate, review cycles, and test pass rates, and use data to improve spec quality and prompt patterns.What You Will BringMust Have Experience:Structured Specification Authoring: Demonstrated ability to write precise, unambiguous feature specifications using BDD/Gherkin, frontmatter markdown, or equivalent formal formats.AI Prompt Engineering: Hands-on experience crafting and refining prompts for LLMs (Claude, GPT, or similar) in a software delivery or product context.Technical Product Ownership: 8+ years in a BA, product owner, or technical PM role working closely with software engineering teams on complex enterprise applications.Node.js/Java Backend Literacy: Sufficient technical depth to understand API contracts, service boundaries, data models, and backend conventions across Node.js and Java to write items.descriptionHtml:
Job Title: AI Solution Owner / Product Owner.

Location: Sattva Magnificia, Old Madras Road, Bangalore.


The InRhythm Opportunity

InRhythm is establishing a modern software engineering practice built around an AI-driven Development Lifecycle (AI-DLC) methodology — spec-driven, agentic, and powered by Claude. As an AI Solution Owner, you will be the engine behind the pod’s delivery velocity, sitting at the intersection of business requirements and AI-augmented engineering. You will translate product intent into precisely structured specifications that Claude can act on with minimal ambiguity. This is a uniquely technical business role: part product owner, part prompt engineer, part delivery lead.


Your Impact

The velocity and accuracy of the entire AI-DLC pod depends on the quality of your specifications. Poorly structured requirements produce mediocre AI output. Your work directly determines how much rework engineers face, how fast features reach production, and how well the agentic pipeline scales as the practice grows. Every spec template, prompt pattern, and context pack you create will become reusable intellectual property for the firm.


What You Will Do

  • Own the Spec Pipeline: Author, maintain, and continuously improve the structured feature specifications that Claude uses to generate code, covering backend APIs, data models, service contracts, and acceptance criteria.
  • Design Spec Templates: Build and evolve reusable specification templates aligned to the Node.js/Java stack, ensuring they surface the context Claude needs including domain model, existing patterns, constraints, and edge cases.
  • Manage the AI-DLC Workflow: Iterate on the end-to-end AI-DLC pipeline from spec ingestion through Claude code generation, human review, and merge governance.
  • Maintain Codebase Context Packs: Own and update the architecture documents, coding convention guides, glossaries, and domain model summaries that are injected into Claude’s context window.
  • Prioritize AI-Suitable Work: Assess the feature backlog for agentic suitability, identifying which features Claude can lead on, which require more human involvement, and why.
  • Measure and Improve: Track agent output quality metrics including rework rate, review cycles, and test pass rates, and use data to improve spec quality and prompt patterns.


What You Will Bring

Must Have Experience:

  • Structured Specification Authoring: Demonstrated ability to write precise, unambiguous feature specifications using BDD/Gherkin, frontmatter markdown, or equivalent formal formats.
  • AI Prompt Engineering: Hands-on experience crafting and refining prompts for LLMs (Claude, GPT, or similar) in a software delivery or product context.
  • Technical Product Ownership: 8+ years in a BA, product owner, or technical PM role working closely with software engineering teams on complex enterprise applications.
  • Node.js/Java Backend Literacy: Sufficient technical depth to understand API contracts, service boundaries, data models, and backend conventions across Node.js and Java to write specs engineers and Claude can act on directly.
  • Analytical Rigor: Ability to decompose complex business requirements into discrete, testable, machine-readable specifications.


Nice to Have Experience:

  • React/Frontend Literacy: Understanding of React component structure, routing, and state management sufficient to spec UI features when needed.
  • Experience building or maintaining prompt libraries and agent workflow tooling.
  • Familiarity with LangChain, LlamaIndex, or similar orchestration frameworks at a conceptual level.
  • Background in enterprise financial services, asset management, or other regulated domains.
  • Experience with context window management strategies for large codebases.
  • Exposure to RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) or compressed context file concepts as applied to codebase documentation.


Compensation

This role aligns with InRhythm’s standard salary bands for Lead Engineers. Specific compensation will be calibrated based on technical depth and experience level.

The InRhythm Consultant

At InRhythm, we are more than technical executors. As a member of the AI-DLC practice, every consultant is expected to demonstrate:

  • Strategic Thinking: The ability to connect business requirements to engineering execution and to see how spec quality drives delivery velocity.
  • Clarity and Ownership: Taking full responsibility for spec quality and communicating trade-offs between agentic automation and manual implementation.
  • Stakeholder Alignment: Building trust with both technical and non-technical stakeholders by ensuring business intent is never lost in the agentic pipeline.


Bias to Measurable Outcomes: A relentless focus on tracking and improving the metrics that demonstrate the value of spec-driven delivery.

items.descriptionHtml:
Purpose

Tata Electronics Private Limited (TEPL) is a greenfield venture of the Tata Group with expertise in manufacturing precision components. Tata Electronics (a wholly owned subsidiary of Tata Sons Pvt. Ltd.) is building India’s first AI-enabled state-of-the-art Semiconductor Foundry. This facility will produce chips for applications such as power management IC, display drivers, microcontrollers (MCU), and high-performance computing logic, addressing the growing demand in markets such as automotive, computing and data storage, wireless communications, and artificial intelligence.

The Tata Group operates in more than 100 countries across six continents, with the mission: 'To improve the quality of life of the communities we serve globally, through long-term stakeholder value creation based on leadership with Trust.'

Key Responsibility

  • Own the product vision and roadmap for GenAI-powered solutions in manufacturing, driving alignment between business goals and technical delivery
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams (engineering, data science, business stakeholders) to define, prioritize, and deliver AI/GenAI features that create measurable impact on manufacturing operations.
  • Translate manufacturing use cases into robust GenAI product requirements, ensuring solutions are scalable, secure, and compliant with enterprise standards.
  • Oversee the end-to-end lifecycle of GenAI product delivery—from ideation and prototyping to production deployment, monitoring, and adoption tracking.
  • Champion the integration of GenAI workflows across manufacturing, supply chain, finance, and shop floor processes for operational excellence.
  • Partner with technical leaders to implement CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure-as-code, and containerized deployments for GenAI applications.
  • Ensure responsible AI adoption by embedding observability, security, and governance frameworks in all GenAI products.

Essential Attributes

  • Proven experience as a product owner or manager in GenAI, LLM architectures, and multi-agent orchestration within manufacturing or enterprise environments.
  • Strong understanding of DevOps, cloud-native and on-prem engineering, and modern AI deployment practices (CI/CD, GitOps, Kubernetes, monitoring).
  • Demonstrated ability to balance technical depth with business impact, driving agile execution in fast-paced, ambiguous settings
  • Passion for digital transformation and shaping Industry 4.0 through GenAI innovation

Qualification

Experience

  • 8–10 years’ experience in product ownership or technical leadership, with at least 3–5 years focused on GenAI and enterprise AI initiatives.
  • Hands-on expertise with GenAI frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, RAG architectures) and LLM deployment strategies.
  • Proficiency with cloud platforms (Nvidia, AWS, Azure, GCP), serverless computing, and container orchestration (EKS, AKS, GKE).
  • Strong understanding of data pipelines, lakehouse/lake architectures, and vector databases (e.g., Pinecone, Milvus, OpenSearch).
  • Experience in manufacturing, semiconductors, or electronics domains is highly desirable.
  • Prior experience in manufacturing, semiconductors, or electronics domains is a strong plus.
  • Certifications in PMP, Agile, SAFe, or Cloud DevOps (AWS/Azure/GCP) are desirable.

Additional Technical Requirements

Competencies

Teamwork

Agility

Customer Empathy

Purposeful Pursuit of Goals

Pioneering

Deep Domain Knowledge

Future Ready

items.title: Senior Manager - Product Marketing
items.description: About the Role We are looking for a Senior Manager of Product Marketing to own positioning, messaging, and go-to-market enablement for one of Aurigo's buyer segments. You will be the primary product marketing owner for your segment, working closely with product managers and the sales team to ensure every buyer interaction is backed by credible, differentiated, and buyer-specific content. This role requires someone who understands B2B enterprise buyers deeply and can translate complex product capabilities into compelling value narratives. Roles and Responsibilities Own positioning, messaging, and value propositions for your assigned buyer segment across all Aurigo products. Develop and maintain segment-specific sales playbooks, battlecards, pitch narratives, and competitive response materials. Partner with product managers to build a deep understanding of buyer jobs to be done, pains, and gains for your segment. Conduct buyer and market research to keep messaging current and grounded in real buyer context. Support the sales team with segment-specific content for RFP responses, demos, and executive presentations. Collaborate with the Senior Manager of Product Enablement to ensure content is operationalised effectively for the field. Manage product launch readiness for your segment and ensure sellers have the messaging, tools, and proof points they need. Own the competitive intelligence process for your segment by monitoring competitor moves and maintaining current battlecards. Work with AI agents to accelerate content production, competitive monitoring, and first-draft collateral generation. Track and report on content utilisation, sales feedback, and win and loss trends for your segment. Soft Skills Strong storytelling ability. Can translate technical product capabilities into buyer-specific value narratives. Excellent written communication. Produces crisp, credible content without extensive editing. Ability to earn trust with sales teams through the relevance and quality of content. Collaborative and responsive in a fast-moving environment. Curiosity about buyer worlds. Reads industry news, attends events, and talks to customers to stay current. Hard Skills Proven ability to develop sales playbooks, battlecards, and go-to-market content for enterprise B2B buyers. Experience conducting buyer research and translating insights into messaging frameworks. Strong understanding of B2B sales cycles and the content needs at each stage. Experience supporting product launches and coordinating cross-functional readiness. Ability to work with AI tools to accelerate content production and competitive research. Technical Skills Familiarity with CRM and sales enablement platforms. Proficiency with content creation and presentation tools. Ability to use AI tools for research synthesis, content drafting, and competitive analysis. Understanding of SaaS product packaging, pricing, and positioning principles. Educational Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field. MBA from a top institution preferred. Prior Experience 8 or more years of experience in product marketing, with at least 3 years in a senior role in B2B SaaS or enterprise software. Demonstrated track record of developing go-to-market content that directly supports sales outcomes. Experience working with or selling to enterprise buyers in capital-intensive industries in the public or commercial sector preferred. Exposure to North American markets and enterprise sales motions. Experience working with AI tools to scale content production is a plus. About Aurigo Aurigo is revolutionizing how the world plans, builds, and manages infrastructure projects with Masterworks, our industry-leading enterprise SaaS platform. Trusted by over 300 customers managing $300 billion in capital programs, Masterworks is setting new standards for project delivery and asset management. Recognized as one of the Top 25 AI Companies of 2024 and a Great Place to Work for three consecutive years, we are leveraging artificial intelligence to create a smarter, more connected future for customers in transportation, water and utilities, healthcare, higher education, and the government, with over 40,000 projects across North America. At Aurigo, we don't just develop software. We shape the future. If you're excited to join a fast-growing company and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry to solve real-world challenges, let's connect.

Job Description

Job Title: Senior Manager – Finance Transformation (Global Product Owner – ATR & OTC)

Location: Bangalore (Onsite)

Reporting to: Senior Director – Finance Operations & Internal Controls


PURPOSE OF ROLE

This role drives global finance transformation across Record‑to‑Report (ATR) and Order‑to‑Cash (OTC) processes by defining product vision, standardizing processes, and enabling touchless, compliant, and scalable finance operations. The role ensures that finance transformation initiatives deliver measurable business value through process simplification, automation, strong controls, and alignment with global finance strategy and policies.


KEY TASKS AND ACCOUNTABILITIES

  • Define and lead the global product vision and roadmap for ATR & OTC finance transformation initiatives aligned with the themes of Touchless, Standard, and Compliant.
  • Drive end‑to‑end ownership of finance transformation programs, from problem definition through solution design, delivery, and value realization.
  • Collaborate with Zone Finance, Shared Service Centers, Global Process Owners, and Technology teams to gather requirements while avoiding local deviations from global standards.
  • Prioritize and manage the product backlog to ensure delivery of the highest‑value features aligned with business priorities and global finance policies.
  • Translate business needs into executable solutions by defining user stories, feature requirements, and acceptance criteria in partnership with business analysts and SMEs.
  • Lead stakeholder engagement across country, zone, and global levels to ensure alignment, buy‑in, and successful adoption of transformation outcomes.
  • Plan and coordinate releases with Corporate Technology teams to ensure timely, high‑quality deployment of finance capabilities.
  • Own the financial governance of transformation initiatives, including business case development, annual planning submissions, and budget adherence.
  • Monitor transformation outcomes using defined KPIs and take corrective actions to ensure target performance is achieved.
  • Identify resistance to change and actively drive change management actions to embed standardized, best‑in‑class finance processes.
  • Provide regular executive updates to senior leadership (VPs, Sr. Directors) on progress, risks, outcomes, and value delivered.


QUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE, SKILLS

Education:

  • Master’s degree in finance, Accounting, Business, Technology, or a related field from a Premium Institute preferred


Experience:

  • 8–12+ years of experience in Finance Operations, Finance Transformation, Process Improvement, or Product Ownership.
  • Strong background in Accounting, Shared Service Center operations, and global finance process standardization.
  • Experience working in SOX‑controlled environments with exposure to internal audit and internal controls.


Technical/Functional Skills:

Mandatory Skills:

  • Finance Process Transformation (ATR / OTC) – Expert
  • Product Ownership & Backlog Management – Advanced
  • Global Stakeholder Management & Influence – Expert
  • Process Standardization & Controls Design – Advanced
  • Business Case & Financial Planning – Advanced
  • SAP Finance (ECC / S4 data model & processes) – Advanced
  • Agile / Scrum Delivery Models – Intermediate


Preferred (Good to have) Skills:

  • Automation & Digital Finance Solutions – Intermediate
  • Generative AI / Agentic AI exposure (opportunities & risks) – Beginner–Intermediate
  • Process Mining & Analytics Platforms – Intermediate
  • Data Analytics & Visualization (Power BI, SQL, Python) – Intermediate
  • Change Management & Adoption Enablement – Intermediate


And above all of this, an undying love for beer!

We dream big to create a future with more cheers.

items.title: Senior Product Owner
items.description: The Senior Product Owner works to fulfill the strategic product roadmap established by the Product Management leadership team and meet business objectives. The person in this position manages multiple teams and backlogs for more than one product and/or for more complex products. This role is responsible for defining the detailed product deliverables. The Senior Product Owner must have a firm understanding of the business domain and product needs taking into account multiple types of users utilizing each solution. This position works closely with engineering on the specifications to ensure their Agile teams have all of the information needed to deliver a product that provides maximum value to the users.Duties & Responsibilities Collaborate with Product Managers to understand the market demands, priorities, and overall product strategy.  Partner with UX and Product Managers on market research efforts to understand and prioritize customer needs.  Work with one or more engineering teams to deliver timely, quality releases that provide high business value and exceed customer expectations.  Flexibility to support more than one product within a portfolio, potentially across value streams  Translate product strategy into detailed requirements and prototypes  Work with the Product Manager and Agile team to define the scope included in a release.  Understand user personas and partner with UX to develop  Create detailed specifications/requirements for the product features so they are clearly understood by the development team.  Write user stories and establish acceptance criteria in collaboration with the team.  Actively participate in PI planning, product sprints and daily stand-up meetings  Research and clarify details as the team develops the application  Be aware of customer commitments in order to communicate internally to assist with managing team expectations  Manage dependencies in and across sprints and releases  Work with the Agile team to manage and prioritize the backlog to ensure alignment with changing business needs.  Prioritize support work as needed  Assist with system status communication and escalation as needed including customer communication  Perform demos to all stakeholders on a regular basis  Keep stakeholders apprised of progress and incorporate their feedback  Understand project budgets, scope and timelines and work to maintain a balance  Use metrics and feedback to improve team and organization processes, best practices, performance, and delivery  May provide work direction or guidance to colleagues with less experience  Act as a product champion within the company Skills Required Strong knowledge of Agile principles and process  Anticipate needs and drive innovation in both business needs and technology enablers.  Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. The Senior Product Owner works to fulfill the strategic product roadmap established by the Product Management leadership team and meet business objectives. The person in this position manages multiple teams and backlogs for more than one product and/or for more complex products. This role is responsible for defining the detailed product deliverables. The Senior Product Owner must have a firm understanding of the business domain and product needs taking into account multiple types of users utilizing each solution. This position works closely with engineering on the specifications to ensure their Agile teams have all of the information needed to deliver a product that provides maximum value to the users.

Duties & Responsibilities

  • Collaborate with Product Managers to understand the market demands, priorities, and overall product strategy.
  • Partner with UX and Product Managers on market research efforts to understand and prioritize customer needs.
  • Work with one or more engineering teams to deliver timely, quality releases that provide high business value and exceed customer expectations.
  • Flexibility to support more than one product within a portfolio, potentially across value streams
  • Translate product strategy into detailed requirements and prototypes
  • Work with the Product Manager and Agile team to define the scope included in a release.
  • Understand user personas and partner with UX to develop
  • Create detailed specifications/requirements for the product features so they are clearly understood by the development team.
  • Write user stories and establish acceptance criteria in collaboration with the team.
  • Actively participate in PI planning, product sprints and daily stand-up meetings
  • Research and clarify details as the team develops the application
  • Be aware of customer commitments in order to communicate internally to assist with managing team expectations
  • Manage dependencies in and across sprints and releases
  • Work with the Agile team to manage and prioritize the backlog to ensure alignment with changing business needs.
  • Prioritize support work as needed
  • Assist with system status communication and escalation as needed including customer communication
  • Perform demos to all stakeholders on a regular basis
  • Keep stakeholders apprised of progress and incorporate their feedback
  • Understand project budgets, scope and timelines and work to maintain a balance
  • Use metrics and feedback to improve team and organization processes, best practices, performance, and delivery
  • May provide work direction or guidance to colleagues with less experience
  • Act as a product champion within the company

Skills Required

  • Strong knowledge of Agile principles and process
  • Anticipate needs and drive innovation in both business needs and technology enablers.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Familiarity with Microsoft SQL for creating queries
  • Ability to influence cross-functional team without formal authority
  • Excellent time management, resource organization and priority establishment skills with the ability to multi-task in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented environment
  • Keen attention to detail
  • Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with diplomacy and tact, including when responding to resistance
  • Ability to work with an optimistic attitude both independently and as part of a team
  • Self-motivated with strong, proven, analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Understands the importance of data flow, data integrity, and business process efficiency, as well as the potential role technology could play to improve or enhance them
  • Works independently with limited guidance from manager
  • Project management skills

items.descriptionHtml:
Why Harvey

At Harvey, we’re transforming how legal and professional services operate. By combining frontier agentic AI, an enterprise-grade platform, and deep domain expertise, we’re reshaping how critical knowledge work gets done for decades to come.

This is a rare chance to help build a generational company at a true inflection point. With 1500+ customers in 60+ countries, strong product-market fit, and world-class investor support, we’re scaling fast and defining a new category in real time. The work is ambitious, the bar is high, and the opportunity for growth — personal, professional, and financial — is unmatched.

Our team moves fast, takes ownership, and is deeply committed to the mission — operating with intensity, staying close to our customers, and pushing each other for excellence. We live by three values: Decisiveness, Simplicity, and Job's Not Finished. We act quickly on clear judgment over perfect information, we believe simplicity is what scales, and we're never satisfied with where we are. If you want to do the best work of your career alongside people who share that drive, we'd love to build with you.

At Harvey, the future of professional services is being written today — and we’re just getting started.

Role Overview

Harvey is building AI that transforms how the world's most sophisticated professionals work. We are looking for a Group Product Manager based in Bengaluru to drive the strategy, development, and delivery of Harvey's Knowledge Platform — the systems that connect Harvey to the legal world's content and tools. This means owning how we build and scale our ecosystem of third-party connectors and data partner integrations that bring rich legal context into Harvey, strengthening core user engagement. This is Harvey's first PM hire in India and a foundational role: you will own a critical product area end-to-end, work closely with our India Engineering team and cross-functional partners across time zones, and shape how Harvey builds and ships products. You will collaborate daily with Engineering, Design, Business Development, Legal Engineering, and GTM teams.

What You'll Do

  • Develop the product roadmap for Harvey's integrations and Knowledge Platform, defining vision, strategy, and priorities across document management systems, the legal tech connector ecosystem, and private legal data.
  • Partner closely with Harvey's India Engineering team to scope, design, and ship high-quality integrations and platform experiences at a rapid pace
  • Drive the strategy for expanding Harvey's connector ecosystem: integration aggregators and self-serve pathways that allow customers and partners to connect their own tools to Harvey at scale.
  • Work closely with business development to engage with partners and manage complex joint developments
  • Deeply understand the workflows of lawyers at leading law firms and in-house legal teams, translating that understanding into product decisions, especially around how enterprise knowledge (DMS files, matter data, firm content) flows into and shapes AI-powered workflows.
  • Act as a bridge between India and HQ (San Francisco / New York), ensuring tight communication loops and strategic alignment across sites.
  • Contribute to shaping Harvey's India product culture — setting the bar for how PMs operate in the region.

What You Have

  • 15+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years at a Staff or Senior PM level at a high-growth technology company.
  • Demonstrated ability to own and drive a product area end-to-end, from strategy through execution and iteration.
  • Strong technical fluency — you can have deep, substantive conversations with engineers about system design, data pipelines, API architecture, and infrastructure trade-offs, including topics like vector search, orchestration systems, and sync reliability.
  • Familiarity with enterprise SaaS go-to-market dynamics: customer commitments, partner integrations, access control and governance requirements, and the operational complexity of shipping to large law firms or regulated industries.
  • Track record of effective cross-functional collaboration across Engineering, Design, GTM, and Customer Success — including navigating dependency-heavy roadmaps and partner relationships.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to present to executives and external stakeholders with clarity and confidence.
  • Experience working in distributed or multi-site teams, with comfort operating across time zones.
  • Based in Bengaluru and eligible to work in India. Harvey does not currently offer visa sponsorship for this role.

Nice to Have

  • Experience in legal tech, professional services technology, or regulated industries.
  • Experience as a founding or early PM in a new-market or new-office setting.
  • Familiarity with LLMs, RAG architectures, or AI-native product design, and an intuition for how data quality and pipeline design affect AI output.

Additional Information

  • Work eligibility: Must be authorized to work in India. Visa sponsorship is not available for this role.

Depending on your location, an Applicant Privacy Notice may apply to you. You can find all of our Applicant Privacy Notices [here].

Harvey is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made by emailing accommodations@harvey.ai

items.title: Product Owner
items.description: Manages the implementation of product management vision and strategy. Manages and leads teams across a range of product management activities including UX (user experience), product management, segmentation and research. Manages the prioritisation and development of the set product's roadmap to deliver value to customers and the business. Manages the scope of product launches to deliver key capabilities on time and on budget. Identifies new products or enhancements to existing products by understanding and analysing user needs, BT Group's current product suite and business requirements of internal functions like sales and delivery. Facilitates partnerships with analytics and engineering teams, and interfaces with cross functional stakeholders e.g. executives, sales, product, marketing, legal, and customer success teams to fulfil the product backlog using Scrum/ Agile methodologies. Coordinates teams in the analysis of customer behaviour, developing metrics and cultivating novel techniques to help customers. Drives the development of comprehensive product requirements to instrument new tracking events, build data tools, pipelines, analytic dashboards and visualisations. Ensures product compliance and awareness with local regulations and inputs where appropriate. Champions, continuously develops and shares with team knowledge on emerging trends and changes in product management. Coaches talent, and manages others, to develop capabilities and ensure performance through upskilling, development and recruitment. Implements ways to improve working processes within the area of product management Technology exposure- ServiceNow or any ITSM and orchestration tools, any monitoring and reporting tools like Sevone, Truesight Will be added advantage
